---
title: "Master Thesis"
author: "Caroline Müller"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    code-tools: true
    toc: true
    #embed-resources: true
    self-contained: true   # embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: top
date: "2026-05-14"
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    dpi: 300
---

# 1 Codebook & Research Questions

## 1.1 Research Questions


RQ2: Using AI surrogates, how does the amount of textual information affect between-surrogate variability in LLM scores and their correspondence with questionnaire scores?

- RQ2a (Between-Surrogate Variability): To what extent do text length and number of texts affect between-surrogate variability (SD) in LLM scores per basic need satisfaction dimension?

- RQ2b (Correspondence): To what extent do text length and number of texts affect the magnitude of correlations between LLM scores and questionnaire scores per basic need satisfaction dimension?

RQ3: To what extent do AI surrogate outputs resemble those of human participants in terms of textual properties, score distributions, and LLM–questionnaire correspondence?

- RQ3a (Distributional Equivalence): To what extent do distributional properties (mean, SD) of LLM scores differ between AI surrogate and human participant data? 
- RQ3b (Correspondence): To what extent do correlations between LLM scores and questionnaire scores per basic need satisfaction differ between AI surrogate and human participant data? 


## 1.2 Code Book

- id: participant id
- id_type: participant type, values "AI" or "HU"
- llm_aut_mean | Mean LLM-scored Autonomy across 3 repeated prompts, based on text. | Numeric | 1 (Low) to 5 (High) |
- llm_com_mean | Mean LLM-scored Competence across 3 repeated prompts, based on text. | Numeric | 1 (Low) to 5 (High) |
- llm_rel_mean | Mean LLM-scored Relatedness across 3 repeated prompts, based on text. | Numeric | 1 (Low) to 5 (High) |
- q_aut_mean | Mean of 4 items measuring self-reported Autonomy need satisfaction | Numeric | 1 (Low) to 5 (High) |
- q_com_mean | Mean of 4 items measuring self-reported Competence need satisfaction | Numeric | 1 (Low) to 5 (High) |
- q_rel_mean | Mean of 4 (humans: 5) items measuring self-reported Relatedness need satisfaction | Numeric | 1 (Low) to 5 (High) |
- actual_words



# 2 Preparation (cleaning, libraries, global variables)

2.1 Software Environment

The analysis is conducted using the tidyverse framework for data
manipulation and the psych and performance packages for psychometric
validation. Tables are rendered according to APA 7th edition standards
using flextable and rempsyc.

```{r}

#| label: setup-environment
#| include: false
#| warning: false

# 1. Clean environment ---------------------------------------------------------
graphics.off()

# 2. Load libraries ------------------------------------------------------------
# Note: suppressPackageStartupMessages hides the "attach" noise from the console
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)      
  library(tidyr)
  library(flextable)
  library(rempsyc)    
  library(scales)       
  library(psych)
  library(performance) 
  library(officer)
  library(paletteer)
  library(janitor)
  library(extrafont)
  library(car)          # leveneTest for variance equality tests
  library(boot)         # boot() for bootstrapped CIs
  library(ggridges)     # ridgeline plots

})

# 3. Set seed for bootstrap CIs ------------------------------------------------
set.seed(2026)

# 4. Message output ------------------------------------------------------------
message("Environment cleaned and libraries loaded successfully.")

```

2.2 Global Study Parameters

```{r}

#| label: study-parameters

# Target sample was calculated using a simulation
TARGET_SAMPLE <- 400

# Bootstrap iterations — set low for development runs
# TODO before submission: raise to 5000+ (RQ2 uses 10000 for consistency)
BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES <- 10000
if (BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES < 1000) warning(
  "BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES is low (", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "). Raise before final render."
)


# Font and colors
THESIS_FONT <- "Calibri"
THESIS_FONTSIZE <- 11
THESIS_PALETTE <- "nationalparkcolors::Acadia"

# Define custom palettes
COLOR_AI <- c("Autonomy" = "#084594", "Competence" = "#4292c6", "Relatedness" = "#9ecae1")
COLOR_HU <- c("Autonomy" = "#800026", "Competence" = "#e31a1c", "Relatedness" = "#fcbba1")

# Combine for scale_fill_manual
COLORS <- c(COLOR_HU, COLOR_AI)


# not yet used
COL_AI_SINGLE = "#1F5A71"
COL_HU_SINGLE = "#BE2A16"
COL_NEUTRAL_SINGLE = "#B3B3B3"
COL_GRID = "#E6E6E6"
COL_LBL_INSIDE = "#FFFFFF"
COL_LBL_OUTSIDE = "#B3B3B3"# ? 


COL_DIFF = "#DDA613"
#COL_AI_SHADES = c("#0A5D83", "#2085AB", "#489CC1", "#6DBCE0", "#87D3ED")
#USED: 
COL_AI_SHADES = c("#1F5A71", "#2085AB", "#489CC1", "#6DBCE0", "#9ecae1","#FFFFFF")
COL_HU_SHADES = c("#BE2A16", "#CE5545", "#DC7D71", "#E9A39B", "#F5C9C5","#FFFFFF")

COL_AI_SHADES2 = c("#98A33E", "#B2BB61", "#CCD485", "#E6ECA9", "#F3F6D4","#FFFFFF")


COL_AI_LINE = COL_AI_SHADES[1]
COL_HU_LINE = COL_HU_SHADES[1]

COL_AI_LLM = COL_AI_SHADES[1]
COL_AI_Q = COL_AI_SHADES2[1]

# 4. AI surrogates colors  -----------------------------------------------------

# used as shades for the 3 text lengths
COL_AI_TEXTS = c("1" = COL_AI_SHADES[4], "3" = COL_AI_SHADES[2], "5" = COL_AI_SHADES[1])

# used as shades for the 3 target word lengths
COL_AI_WORDS = c("50" = COL_AI_SHADES[4], "150" = COL_AI_SHADES[2], "300" = COL_AI_SHADES[1])

# 5. LABELS --------------------------------------------------------------------

# Labels for the basic psychological needs
AUT <- "autonomy"
COM <- "competence"
REL <- "relatedness"

TABLE_LABEL_AUT <- "Autonomy"
TABLE_LABEL_COM <- "Competence"
TABLE_LABEL_REL <- "Relatedness"
#NEEDS <- c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)
#ITEMS <- paste0(rep(NEEDS, each = 4), " ", 1:4) # Generate the labels

# Labels for LLM vs Questionnaire comparison
LABEL_WORDC      <- "Word Count (LLM)"
LABEL_NEEDS <- paste("Need Dimension")
LABEL_LLM_AUT <- paste("LLM", TABLE_LABEL_AUT)
LABEL_LLM_COM <- paste("LLM", TABLE_LABEL_COM)
LABEL_LLM_REL <- paste("LLM", TABLE_LABEL_REL)
LABEL_Q_AUT   <- paste("Questionnaire", TABLE_LABEL_AUT)
LABEL_Q_COM   <- paste("Questionnaire", TABLE_LABEL_COM)
LABEL_Q_REL   <- paste("Questionnaire", TABLE_LABEL_REL)

# Labels for samples
LABEL_SAMPLE <- "Sample"
LABEL_AI <- "AI surrogates"
LABEL_HU <- "Humans"

# Confidence interval level
CI_LEVEL <- .95
CI_LABEL <- paste0(CI_LEVEL * 100, "% CI")

```

2.3 Helper functions

For APA7 styling.

```{r}

#| label: helper-functions
#| include: false

# Define a standard APA 7 style for this thesis --------------------------------
style_apa7 <- function(x) {
  x |>
    font(fontname = THESIS_FONT, part = "all") |>
    fontsize(size = THESIS_FONTSIZE, part = "all") |>
    valign(valign = "top", part = "body") |>
    set_table_properties(layout = "autofit")
}

# Generic APA note helper (refined for internal use) ---------------------------
add_apa_note <- function(x, note_text) {
  x |>
    add_footer_lines("") |>
    mk_par(
      part = "footer", i = 1, j = 1,
      value = as_paragraph(
        as_chunk("Note. ", props = fp_text_default(italic = TRUE,
                                                   font.family = THESIS_FONT,
                                                   font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
        as_chunk(note_text, props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE,
                                                    font.family = THESIS_FONT,
                                                    font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE))
      )
    )
}

```

# 3 Prep Data


## 3.1 Import human data

```{r}
# 1. Setup Paths and Import ----------------------------------------------------
data_path_human <- "../10_data/10_raw/humans_2026-04-13.csv"

d_human_raw <- read.csv(data_path_human, sep = "|", na.strings = "NA")



message("Data loaded: ", basename(data_path_human), " | Modified: ",
        format(file.mtime(data_path_human), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"))



# 2. Rename and Clean ----------------------------------------------------------
# All columns named explicitly — any upstream rename breaks loudly here.




colSums(is.na(d_human_raw))

d_human <- d_human_raw |>
  rename(
    # Demographics
    id             = participant_id,
    gender         = gender,
    age_year       = birthyear,
    edu_level      = education,
    
    # SDT Self-Report Items
    aut_1 = aut1, aut_2 = aut2, aut_3 = aut3, aut_4 = aut4,
    com_1 = comp1, com_2 = comp2, com_3 = comp3, com_4 = comp4,
    rel_1 = rel1, rel_2 = rel2, rel_3 = rel3, rel_4 = rel4, rel_5 = rel5,
    
    # LLM Ratings (Validation Targets)
    llm_aut_1 = llm_aut_rep1, llm_aut_2 = llm_aut_rep2, llm_aut_3 = llm_aut_rep3,
    llm_com_1 = llm_com_rep1, llm_com_2 = llm_com_rep2, llm_com_3 = llm_com_rep3,
    llm_rel_1 = llm_rel_rep1, llm_rel_2 = llm_rel_rep2, llm_rel_3 = llm_rel_rep3,
    
    # word count
    actual_words = wc_total
  ) |>
  # Drop PVQ items before type conversion to save memory/processing
  select(-starts_with("PVQ")) |>
  mutate(
    across(c(id, gender, edu_level), as.factor),
    age_year = as.integer(age_year),
    across(starts_with("aut_"), as.numeric),
    across(starts_with("com_"), as.numeric),
    across(starts_with("rel_"), as.numeric),
    across(starts_with("llm_"), as.numeric)
  ) |>
  # Compute means and reshape to match d_ai structure
  mutate(
    llm_aut_mean = rowMeans(across(c(llm_aut_1, llm_aut_2, llm_aut_3)), na.rm = TRUE),
    llm_com_mean = rowMeans(across(c(llm_com_1, llm_com_2, llm_com_3)), na.rm = TRUE),
    llm_rel_mean = rowMeans(across(c(llm_rel_1, llm_rel_2, llm_rel_3)), na.rm = TRUE),
    q_aut_mean   = rowMeans(across(c(aut_1, aut_2, aut_3, aut_4)),       na.rm = TRUE),
    q_com_mean   = rowMeans(across(c(com_1, com_2, com_3, com_4)),       na.rm = TRUE),
    q_rel_mean   = rowMeans(across(c(rel_1, rel_2, rel_3, rel_4, rel_5)), na.rm = TRUE),
    id_type      = "HU",
    actual_words = as.integer(actual_words)
  ) |>
  select(id, id_type, llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
         q_aut_mean, q_com_mean, q_rel_mean, actual_words)



```


## 3.2 Import AI data

The dataset, containing both human and AI surrogate responses, was
generated via a Python-based pipeline and exported to CSV. 

```{r}

#| label: import-validation
#| message: false


# 1. Setup Paths and Import ----------------------------------------------------
data_path_ai <- "../10_data/80_analyses_exports/d_rq3_ai.rds"

d_ai_raw <- readRDS(data_path_ai)


# 2. Rename and Clean ----------------------------------------------------------
d_ai <- d_ai_raw |>
  rename(
    id = id,
    id_type = id_type, 
    llm_aut_mean = llm_aut_mean,
    llm_com_mean = llm_com_mean,
    llm_rel_mean = llm_rel_mean,
    q_aut_mean = q_aut_mean, 
    q_com_mean = q_com_mean, 
    q_rel_mean = q_rel_mean, 
    actual_words = actual_words,
    condition = condition,
    text_count = text_count,
    target_words = target_words
  ) |>
  mutate(
    actual_words = as.integer(actual_words),
    llm_aut_mean = as.numeric(llm_aut_mean),
    llm_com_mean = as.numeric(llm_com_mean),
    llm_rel_mean = as.numeric(llm_rel_mean),
    q_aut_mean = as.numeric(q_aut_mean),
    q_com_mean = as.numeric(q_com_mean),
    q_rel_mean = as.numeric(q_rel_mean),
    condition = as.factor(condition), 
    target_words = as.integer(target_words),
    text_count   = as.integer(text_count)
    
  ) |>
  select(id, id_type, llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
         q_aut_mean, q_com_mean, q_rel_mean, actual_words)


# 3. Structural Integrity Check ------------------------------------------------

# Ensure all variables required for subsequent analyses are present 
required_vars <- c("id", "id_type",
                   "llm_aut_mean", "llm_com_mean", "llm_rel_mean",
                   "q_aut_mean",   "q_com_mean",   "q_rel_mean",
                   "actual_words")
missing_vars  <- setdiff(required_vars, names(d_ai))

if (length(missing_vars) > 0) {
  stop(paste("CRITICAL: Missing columns:", paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", ")))
}

```


## 3.3 Import RQ2 exports

Pre-computed objects exported from `10_Analyses_RQ2.qmd`. Re-render that file first if the source data changes.

```{r}
#| label: import-rq2-exports
#| include: false

exports_dir <- "../10_data/80_analyses_exports/"

# d_ai_conditions: AI surrogate data for all 9 conditions (3 word lengths × 3 text counts).
# Same structure as d_ai but not filtered. Columns: id, id_type, llm_*_mean, q_*_mean,
# actual_words, condition, text_count, target_words. Use for condition-level comparisons with humans.
d_ai_conditions <- readRDS(paste0(exports_dir, "d_rq3_ai_conditions.rds"))

# tbl_icc_raw: LLM test-retest reliability (ICC 2,1) per condition × need dimension.
# Computed in 10_Analyses_RQ2.qmd section 7.2 via psych::ICC across 3 scoring repeats.
# Columns: Condition, Need (llm_aut/com/rel), ICC, CI_lo, CI_hi. Use for reliability benchmarking.
tbl_icc_raw <- readRDS(paste0(exports_dir, "tbl_icc_raw.rds"))

# tbl_word_count_by_condition: descriptive word count stats per condition.
# Columns: condition, target_words, text_count, mean_words, sd_words, n.
# Use for verifying instructional fidelity and reporting word counts in RQ3 context.
tbl_word_count_by_condition <- readRDS(paste0(exports_dir, "tbl_word_count_by_condition.rds"))

message("RQ2 exports loaded: d_ai_conditions (", nrow(d_ai_conditions), " rows), ",
        "tbl_icc_raw (", nrow(tbl_icc_raw), " rows), ",
        "tbl_word_count_by_condition (", nrow(tbl_word_count_by_condition), " rows)")
```

## 3.4 Quick integrity check

The dataset, containing both human and AI surrogate responses, was
generated via a Python-based pipeline and exported to CSV. The following
block imports this data and lists already some basic data integrity checks

```{r}

#| label: audit-check-ai
#| message: false

# Audit Summary -------------------------------------------------------------
tbl_import_audit_ai <- tibble::tribble(
  ~Metric,                ~Value,
  "Source File",          basename(data_path_ai),
  "Dataframe",            as.character(deparse(substitute(d_ai))),
  "Total Rows",           as.character(nrow(d_ai)),
  "Distinct Personas",    as.character(dplyr::n_distinct(d_ai$id)),
  "Columns",              as.character(ncol(d_ai))
)

tbl_import_audit_ai


```


```{r}
#| label: audit-check-human
#| message: false

# Audit Summary -------------------------------------------------------------
tbl_import_audit_human <- tibble::tribble(
  ~Metric,                 ~Value,
  "Source File",           data_path_human,
  "Dataframe",             as.character(deparse(substitute(d_human))),
  "Total Rows",            as.character(nrow(d_human)),
  "Distinct Participants", as.character(dplyr::n_distinct(d_human$id)),
  "Columns",               as.character(ncol(d_human))
)

tbl_import_audit_human

```


```{r}

#| label: import-debug
#| include: false
#| message: false

# looking at the "fresh" state of d before any column mutations

message("--- START DEBUG LOG: Section 3.1 ---")
message("Columns AI:     ", ncol(d_ai))
message("Columns Humans:     ", ncol(d_human))

# 1. Structural Inspection -----------------------------------------------------
#glimpse(d)

# 2. Integrity Check (Internal logic) -------------------------------------------
# Check for any immediate NAs in key variables 
colSums(is.na(d_ai))
colSums(is.na(d_human))


# 3. Clean-up verification umns ------------------------------------------------
message("--- END DEBUG LOG: Section 3.1 ---")

```

## 3.5 Merge AI and Human data

```{r}
#| label: merge-ai-human
#| include: false

d <- dplyr::bind_rows(d_ai, d_human)

stopifnot(
  nrow(d) == nrow(d_ai) + nrow(d_human)
)

message(
  "Merged: ", 
  nrow(d_ai), " AI rows + ",
  nrow(d_human), " human rows = ", 
  nrow(d), " total"
)

```

## 3.6 Matched Condition Verification

The matched condition is the AI surrogate condition whose cumulative word length is closest to the average total word length of human texts. Text count is held constant at 3; the short-3 condition (target ~150 words per text × 3 texts = ~450 words total) is the matched condition. Both groups: `actual_words` = total word count across 3 texts.

```{r}
#| label: integrity-check-merge
#| include: false


stopifnot(
  n_distinct(d$id_type) == 2,
  "AI" %in% d$id_type,
  "HU" %in% d$id_type,
  !any(is.na(d$actual_words)),
  !any(is.na(d$llm_aut_mean)), !any(is.na(d$llm_com_mean)), !any(is.na(d$llm_rel_mean)),
  !any(is.na(d$q_aut_mean)),   !any(is.na(d$q_com_mean)),   !any(is.na(d$q_rel_mean))
)
message("Integrity check passed: both id_type groups present, no NAs in key variables.")
```


```{r}
#| label: tbl-wordcount-match
#| tbl-cap: "Total Word Count Summary by Sample"
#| echo: false

# Word count summary per group
check_wordcount_match <- d |>
  group_by(id_type) |>
  summarise(
    n   = n(),
    M   = round(mean(actual_words), 2),
    SD  = round(sd(actual_words), 2),
    Mdn = round(median(actual_words, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Min = min(actual_words),
    Max = max(actual_words),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Word count summary table
tbl_wordcount_match <- check_wordcount_match |>
  mutate(id_type = c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)]) |>
  rename(!!LABEL_SAMPLE := id_type, N = n) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_header_row(values = c("", "Actual word count summary"), colwidths = c(2, 5)) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "Actual words are the total word count across 3 scored texts ",
     "for both samples. ",
    "For AI surrogates, short-3 is the matched condition."
  ))

tbl_wordcount_match
```


# 4 Descriptives: Table

Descriptive statistics per need dimension and score type (LLM-scored vs. questionnaire-based), reported separately for AI surrogates and human participants. All variables are on a 1–5 scale.

```{r}
#| label: desc-integrity-check
#| include: false

stopifnot(
  all(!is.na(d$llm_aut_mean)), all(!is.na(d$q_aut_mean)),
  all(!is.na(d$llm_com_mean)), all(!is.na(d$q_com_mean)),
  all(!is.na(d$llm_rel_mean)), all(!is.na(d$q_rel_mean)),
  nrow(d[d$id_type == "AI", ]) > 0,
  nrow(d[d$id_type == "HU", ]) > 0
)
message("Descriptives integrity check passed.")
```




```{r}
#| label: tbl-desc-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Descriptive Statistics for LLM Scores and Questionnaire Scores by Need Dimension and Group (AI Surrogates vs. Human Participants)"
#| echo: false

# Layer B: descriptive summary — inline pivot, no persistent d_long
tbl_desc_summary <- d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
                  q_aut_mean,   q_com_mean,   q_rel_mean),
    names_to  = "variable",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Need = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "q_aut_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      variable %in% c("llm_com_mean", "q_com_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      variable %in% c("llm_rel_mean", "q_rel_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    `Score Type` = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "llm_com_mean", "llm_rel_mean") ~ "LLM Score",
      variable %in% c("q_aut_mean",   "q_com_mean",   "q_rel_mean")   ~ "Questionnaire Score"
    )
  ) |>
  group_by(id_type, Need, `Score Type`) |>
  summarise(
    M   = mean(score,   na.rm = TRUE),
    SD  = sd(score,     na.rm = TRUE),
    Mdn = round(median(score, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Min = round(min(score,    na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Max = round(max(score,    na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    n   = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(Need, `Score Type`, id_type) |>
  mutate(
    id_type = c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)],
    m_sd    = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", M, SD)
  ) |>
  rename(!!LABEL_SAMPLE := id_type) |>
  select(Need, `Score Type`, !!LABEL_SAMPLE, m_sd, Mdn, Min, Max) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  merge_v(j = c("Need", "Score Type")) |>
  mk_par(i = 1, j = "m_sd", part = "header", value = as_paragraph(
    as_chunk("M",  props = fp_text_default(italic = TRUE,  font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk(" (", props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE, font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk("SD", props = fp_text_default(italic = TRUE,  font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk(")",  props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE, font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE))
  )) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "All scores on a 1–5 scale. LLM Score = mean of 3 scoring repeats. ",
    "Questionnaire Score = mean of 4 items. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "AI", ]), "; ",
    LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "HU", ]), "."
  ))

tbl_desc_summary
```


```{r}
#| label: tbl-desc-summary-wide
#| tbl-cap: "Descriptive Statistics for LLM Scores and Questionnaire Scores by Need Dimension and Group (M (SD))"
#| echo: false

# Wide format: one M (SD) column per group + Δ M (Δ SD) — for comparison with original tbl-desc-summary
tbl_desc_summary_wide <- d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
                  q_aut_mean,   q_com_mean,   q_rel_mean),
    names_to  = "variable",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Need = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "q_aut_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      variable %in% c("llm_com_mean", "q_com_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      variable %in% c("llm_rel_mean", "q_rel_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Instrument = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "llm_com_mean", "llm_rel_mean") ~ "LLM Score",
      variable %in% c("q_aut_mean",   "q_com_mean",   "q_rel_mean")   ~ "Questionnaire Score"
    )
  ) |>
  group_by(id_type, Need, Instrument) |>
  summarise(
    m  = mean(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(score,   na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = id_type, values_from = c(m, sd)) |>
  arrange(Need, Instrument) |>
  transmute(
    Need,
    Instrument,
    hu_col    = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", m_HU, sd_HU),
    ai_col    = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", m_AI, sd_AI),
    delta_col = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", m_AI - m_HU, sd_AI - sd_HU)
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  merge_v(j = "Need") |>
  set_header_labels(Need = LABEL_NEEDS, hu_col = "M (SD)", ai_col = "M (SD)", delta_col = "Δ M (Δ SD)") |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "", LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI, "Difference"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L)
  ) |>
  mk_par(i = 2, j = 3, part = "header", value = as_paragraph(
    as_chunk("M",   props = fp_text_default(italic = TRUE,  font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk(" (",  props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE, font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk("SD",  props = fp_text_default(italic = TRUE,  font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk(")",   props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE, font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE))
  )) |>
  mk_par(i = 2, j = 4, part = "header", value = as_paragraph(
    as_chunk("M",   props = fp_text_default(italic = TRUE,  font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk(" (",  props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE, font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk("SD",  props = fp_text_default(italic = TRUE,  font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk(")",   props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE, font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE))
  )) |>
  mk_par(i = 2, j = 5, part = "header", value = as_paragraph(
    as_chunk("Δ ",  props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE, font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk("M",   props = fp_text_default(italic = TRUE,  font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk(" (Δ ", props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE, font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk("SD",  props = fp_text_default(italic = TRUE,  font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE)),
    as_chunk(")",   props = fp_text_default(italic = FALSE, font.family = THESIS_FONT, font.size = THESIS_FONTSIZE))
  )) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "Values are M (SD). All scores on a 1–5 scale. ",
    "LLM = mean of 3 scoring repeats; Questionnaire = mean of 4–5 items. ",
    "Δ = AI surrogates − Humans. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "AI", ]), "; ",
    LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "HU", ]), "."
  ))

tbl_desc_summary_wide
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-desc-iqr-range
#| tbl-cap: "Descriptive Statistics for LLM Scores and Questionnaire Scores by Need Dimension and Group (IQR and Range)"
#| echo: false

tbl_desc_iqr_range <- d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
                  q_aut_mean,   q_com_mean,   q_rel_mean),
    names_to  = "variable",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Need = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "q_aut_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      variable %in% c("llm_com_mean", "q_com_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      variable %in% c("llm_rel_mean", "q_rel_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Instrument = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "llm_com_mean", "llm_rel_mean") ~ "LLM Score",
      variable %in% c("q_aut_mean",   "q_com_mean",   "q_rel_mean")   ~ "Questionnaire Score"
    )
  ) |>
  group_by(id_type, Need, Instrument) |>
  summarise(
    iqr   = IQR(score,           na.rm = TRUE),
    range = max(score, na.rm = TRUE) - min(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = id_type, values_from = c(iqr, range)) |>
  arrange(Need, Instrument) |>
  transmute(
    Need,
    Instrument,
    hu_iqr   = sprintf("%.2f", iqr_HU),
    ai_iqr   = sprintf("%.2f", iqr_AI),
    hu_range = sprintf("%.2f", range_HU),
    ai_range = sprintf("%.2f", range_AI)
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  merge_v(j = "Need") |>
  set_header_labels(
    Need       = LABEL_NEEDS,
    Instrument = "Score Type",
    hu_iqr     = LABEL_HU,
    ai_iqr     = LABEL_AI,
    hu_range   = LABEL_HU,
    ai_range   = LABEL_AI
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "", "IQR", "Range"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 1L, 2L, 2L)
  ) |>
  align(i = 1, align = "center", part = "header") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "IQR = interquartile range (Q3 − Q1). Range = maximum − minimum. ",
    "All scores on a 1–5 scale. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "AI", ]), "; ",
    LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "HU", ]), "."
  ))

tbl_desc_iqr_range
```

### Kurtosis, Skewness

Interpretation Kurtosis
- Excess kurtosis near 0: roughly normal tail behavior
- Positive excess kurtosis: more outlier-prone, heavier tails
- Negative excess kurtosis: flatter / lighter-tailed / more bounded

Interpretation Skewness
- Around 0: little asymmetry
- Larger positive values: increasing right-tail pull; the mean is often above the median
- Larger negative values: increasing left-tail pull; the mean is often below the median

```{r}
#| label: tbl-desc-distrib
#| tbl-cap: "Distribution Statistics for LLM Scores and Questionnaire Scores by Need Dimension and Group (Quantiles, IQR, Skewness, Kurtosis)"
#| echo: false

tbl_desc_distrib <- d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
                  q_aut_mean,   q_com_mean,   q_rel_mean),
    names_to  = "variable",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Need = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "q_aut_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      variable %in% c("llm_com_mean", "q_com_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      variable %in% c("llm_rel_mean", "q_rel_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Instrument = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "llm_com_mean", "llm_rel_mean") ~ "LLM Score",
      variable %in% c("q_aut_mean",   "q_com_mean",   "q_rel_mean")   ~ "Questionnaire Score"
    )
  ) |>
  group_by(id_type, Need, Instrument) |>
  summarise(
    q1   = round(quantile(score, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    q3   = round(quantile(score, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    iqr  = round(IQR(score, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    skew = round(psych::skew(score), 2),
    kurt = round(psych::kurtosi(score), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = id_type, values_from = c(q1, q3, iqr, skew, kurt)) |>
  arrange(Need, Instrument) |>
  select(Need, Instrument,
         q1_HU, q3_HU, iqr_HU, skew_HU, kurt_HU,
         q1_AI, q3_AI, iqr_AI, skew_AI, kurt_AI) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  merge_v(j = "Need") |>
  set_header_labels(
    Need        = LABEL_NEEDS,
    Instrument  = "Instrument",
    q1_HU       = "Q1",
    q3_HU       = "Q3",
    iqr_HU      = "IQR",
    skew_HU     = "Skewness",
    kurt_HU     = "Kurtosis",
    q1_AI       = "Q1",
    q3_AI       = "Q3",
    iqr_AI      = "IQR",
    skew_AI     = "Skewness",
    kurt_AI     = "Kurtosis"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", "", LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI),
    colwidths = c(1L, 1L, 5L, 5L)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "All scores on a 1–5 scale. ",
    "Q1 = 25th percentile; Q3 = 75th percentile; IQR = interquartile range (Q3 − Q1); ",
    "Skewness = Fisher's moment coefficient (g1); Kurtosis = excess kurtosis (g2). ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "AI", ]), "; ",
    LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "HU", ]), "."
  ))

tbl_desc_distrib
```

### Correlations

```{r}
#| label: tbl-desc-corr
#| tbl-cap: "Pearson Correlations Between LLM and Questionnaire Scores by Need Dimension and Group"
#| echo: false


# The !! operator on TABLE_LABEL_AUT/COM/REL and LABEL_SAMPLE means 
# all column headers come from globals.

corr_ai <- d |> dplyr::filter(id_type == "AI") |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    !!TABLE_LABEL_AUT := round(cor(llm_aut_mean, q_aut_mean), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_COM := round(cor(llm_com_mean, q_com_mean), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_REL := round(cor(llm_rel_mean, q_rel_mean), 2)
  )
corr_hu <- d |> dplyr::filter(id_type == "HU") |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    !!TABLE_LABEL_AUT := round(cor(llm_aut_mean, q_aut_mean), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_COM := round(cor(llm_com_mean, q_com_mean), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_REL := round(cor(llm_rel_mean, q_rel_mean), 2)
  )

tbl_desc_corr <- tibble::tibble(
  Need            = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT,            TABLE_LABEL_COM,            TABLE_LABEL_REL),
  !!LABEL_HU     := c(corr_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]], corr_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]], corr_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]]),
  !!LABEL_AI     := c(corr_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]], corr_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]], corr_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]])
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(`Δr` = round(.data[[LABEL_AI]] - .data[[LABEL_HU]], 2)) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "Pearson r between LLM score (mean of 3 repeats) and questionnaire score (mean of 4 items) ",
    "per need dimension. ",
    "Δr = r(AI surrogates) − r(Human). ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "AI", ]), "; ",
    LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d[d$id_type == "HU", ]), "."
  ))

tbl_desc_corr
```
# 5 Descriptives: Plots



### Scatter plot 

```{r}
#| label: debug-scatter-pairs
#| include: false

# z-transform

# Extract by group
l_aut_ai <- d$llm_aut_mean[d$id_type == "AI"]
l_com_ai <- d$llm_com_mean[d$id_type == "AI"]
l_rel_ai <- d$llm_rel_mean[d$id_type == "AI"]
l_aut_hu <- d$llm_aut_mean[d$id_type == "HU"]
l_com_hu <- d$llm_com_mean[d$id_type == "HU"]
l_rel_hu <- d$llm_rel_mean[d$id_type == "HU"]

q_aut_ai <- d$q_aut_mean[d$id_type == "AI"]
q_com_ai <- d$q_com_mean[d$id_type == "AI"]
q_rel_ai <- d$q_rel_mean[d$id_type == "AI"]
q_aut_hu <- d$q_aut_mean[d$id_type == "HU"]
q_com_hu <- d$q_com_mean[d$id_type == "HU"]
q_rel_hu <- d$q_rel_mean[d$id_type == "HU"]

# Z-score each vector
z_l_aut_ai <- (l_aut_ai - mean(l_aut_ai)) / sd(l_aut_ai)
z_l_com_ai <- (l_com_ai - mean(l_com_ai)) / sd(l_com_ai)
z_l_rel_ai <- (l_rel_ai - mean(l_rel_ai)) / sd(l_rel_ai)
z_l_aut_hu <- (l_aut_hu - mean(l_aut_hu)) / sd(l_aut_hu)
z_l_com_hu <- (l_com_hu - mean(l_com_hu)) / sd(l_com_hu)
z_l_rel_hu <- (l_rel_hu - mean(l_rel_hu)) / sd(l_rel_hu)

z_q_aut_ai <- (q_aut_ai - mean(q_aut_ai)) / sd(q_aut_ai)
z_q_com_ai <- (q_com_ai - mean(q_com_ai)) / sd(q_com_ai)
z_q_rel_ai <- (q_rel_ai - mean(q_rel_ai)) / sd(q_rel_ai)
z_q_aut_hu <- (q_aut_hu - mean(q_aut_hu)) / sd(q_aut_hu)
z_q_com_hu <- (q_com_hu - mean(q_com_hu)) / sd(q_com_hu)
z_q_rel_hu <- (q_rel_hu - mean(q_rel_hu)) / sd(q_rel_hu)



# Scatter plot matrix, just to show that they linearly related

# pairs(~ fahr + celc + z)

pairs(~ l_aut_ai + q_aut_ai +  z_l_aut_ai + z_q_aut_ai)

#pairs(~ z_l_aut_ai + z_q_aut_ai)

rm(l_aut_ai, l_com_ai, l_rel_ai, l_aut_hu, l_com_hu, l_rel_hu,
   q_aut_ai, q_com_ai, q_rel_ai, q_aut_hu, q_com_hu, q_rel_hu,
   z_l_aut_ai, z_l_com_ai, z_l_rel_ai, z_l_aut_hu, z_l_com_hu, z_l_rel_hu,
   z_q_aut_ai, z_q_com_ai, z_q_rel_ai, z_q_aut_hu, z_q_com_hu, z_q_rel_hu)

```

## Scatter plot - ggplot

```{r}
#| label: fig-desc-corr-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Scatter plots of LLM mean scores (x-axis) against questionnaire composite scores (y-axis), faceted by need dimension (columns) and sample group (rows). Top row: AI surrogates; bottom row: human participants Each point represents one participant. Lines show OLS regression fits with 95% CIs. Pearson r values are shown per panel and correspond to those in the table above."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-dpi: 300

corr_labels_scatter <- d |>
  group_by(id_type) |>
  summarise(
    aut = round(cor(llm_aut_mean, q_aut_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    com = round(cor(llm_com_mean, q_com_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    rel = round(cor(llm_rel_mean, q_rel_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(aut, com, rel), names_to = "need", values_to = "r") |>
  mutate(
    Need  = case_when(
      need == "aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      need == "com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      need == "rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Group = c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)],
    label = paste0("r = ", sprintf("%.2f", r))
  )

d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols          = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
                      q_aut_mean,   q_com_mean,   q_rel_mean),
    names_to      = c("source", "need"),
    names_pattern = "(llm|q)_(aut|com|rel)_mean"
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = source, values_from = value) |>
  mutate(
    Need  = case_when(
      need == "aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      need == "com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      need == "rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Group = c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)]
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = llm, y = q, color = Group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(
    data       = corr_labels_scatter,
    aes(x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = label, color = Group),
    hjust = -0.15, vjust = 1.5,
    size = 3.5, inherit.aes = FALSE, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  facet_grid(Group ~ Need) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  labs(x = "LLM Score", y = "Questionnaire Score", color = LABEL_SAMPLE) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "none"
  )


```

## Scatter plot - with jitter

```{r}
#| label: fig-desc-corr-scatter-jitter
#| fig-cap: "Scatter plots of LLM mean scores (x-axis) against questionnaire composite scores (y-axis), faceted by need dimension (columns) and sample group (rows). Top row: AI surrogates; bottom row: human participants. Points are jittered to reveal density. Lines show OLS regression fits with 95% CIs. Pearson r values are shown per panel."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-dpi: 600

# 1. Calculate labels with specific positioning coordinates
corr_labels_scatter_jitter <- d |>
  group_by(id_type) |>
  summarise(
    aut = round(cor(llm_aut_mean, q_aut_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    com = round(cor(llm_com_mean, q_com_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    rel = round(cor(llm_rel_mean, q_rel_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(aut, com, rel), names_to = "need", values_to = "r") |>
  mutate(
    Need  = case_when(
      need == "aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      need == "com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      need == "rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Group = c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)],
    label = paste0("r = ", sprintf("%.2f", r)),
    # Use explicit coordinates instead of -Inf to ensure consistent padding
    x_pos = 1.2, 
    y_pos = 4.8  
  )

# 2. Main Plot Pipeline
d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols          = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
                      q_aut_mean,   q_com_mean,   q_rel_mean),
    names_to      = c("source", "need"),
    names_pattern = "(llm|q)_(aut|com|rel)_mean"
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = source, values_from = value) |>
  mutate(
    Need  = factor(case_when(
      need == "aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      need == "com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      need == "rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ), levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    Group = c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)]
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = llm, y = q, color = Group, fill = Group)) +
  # Use jitter to resolve the integer-score overlap
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2, width = 0.15, height = 0.15, stroke = 0) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_text(
    data    = corr_labels_scatter_jitter,
    aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = label, color = Group),
    hjust   = 0, 
    size    = 3.5, 
    inherit.aes = FALSE, 
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  facet_grid(Group ~ Need) +
  # Standardize axes to the 1-5 scale
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.8, 5.2), breaks = 1:5) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.8, 5.2), breaks = 1:5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "AI surrogates" = COL_AI_SHADES[1],
    "Humans"        = COL_HU_SHADES[1]
  )) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "AI surrogates" = COL_AI_SHADES[1],
    "Humans"        = COL_HU_SHADES[1]
  )) +
  labs(
    x = "LLM Predicted Score",
    y = "Human Questionnaire Score",
    color = LABEL_SAMPLE
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey97", color = NA),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.spacing    = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    legend.position  = "none"
  )


```


### five plot types

Five plot types in separate chunks for easy deletion/selection. Histograms (5.1) represent the bounded 1–5 integer scale — recommended for the thesis. Density plots (5.2) smooth the histogram but may show phantom tails outside 1–5. Violin + jitter (5.3) combines distribution shape with individual points. Boxplots (5.4) give a compact group comparison. Ridgeline plots (5.5) are well-suited for overlapping group comparisons. Note: with simulated human data, distributions appear artificially smooth.

```{r}
#| label: plot-setup
#| include: false

# Create d_scores_long once for all section 5 plot chunks; rm() in plot-cleanup
d_scores_long <- d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
                  q_aut_mean,   q_com_mean,   q_rel_mean),
    names_to  = "variable",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Need = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "q_aut_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      variable %in% c("llm_com_mean", "q_com_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      variable %in% c("llm_rel_mean", "q_rel_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    `Score Type` = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "llm_com_mean", "llm_rel_mean") ~ "LLM Score",
      variable %in% c("q_aut_mean",   "q_com_mean",   "q_rel_mean")   ~ "Questionnaire Score"
    ),
    Group = factor(c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)], levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU)),
    Need  = factor(Need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    `Score Type` = factor(`Score Type`, levels = c("LLM Score", "Questionnaire Score"))
  )
```

## 5.1 Histograms

```{r}
#| label: fig-hist
#| fig-cap: "Score distributions by need dimension and group (AI surrogates vs. human participants). Vertical lines indicate group means. Rows: LLM scores (top) and questionnaire scores (bottom)."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

GROUP_COLORS <- setNames(
  c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE),
  c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU)
)

means_df <- d_scores_long |>
  group_by(Group, Need, `Score Type`) |>
  summarise(M = mean(score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

fig_hist <- d_scores_long |>
  ggplot(aes(x = score, fill = Group)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1/3, boundary = 1, position = "dodge", alpha = 0.85, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(data = means_df,
             aes(xintercept = M, color = Group, linetype = Group),
             linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(`Score Type` ~ Need) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = GROUP_COLORS) +
  scale_color_manual(values = GROUP_COLORS) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:5, limits = c(2/3, 5 + 1/3)) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Scores by Need and Group",
       x = "Score (1–5)", y = "Count",
       fill = LABEL_SAMPLE, color = "Group (mean)", linetype = "Group (mean)") +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

fig_hist
```



## 5.2 Density + Rug

Density curves smooth the histogram; they may show phantom tails outside 1–5. Rug marks (bottom) show individual observations — most useful for N < 200; at N ≥ 400 (AI group) tick marks overlap and histograms (5.1) are more informative.

```{r}
#| label: fig-density
#| fig-cap: "Kernel density estimates of score distributions by need dimension and group. Rug marks indicate individual observations. Rows: LLM scores (top) and questionnaire scores (bottom)."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

fig_density <- d_scores_long |>
  ggplot(aes(x = score, fill = Group, color = Group)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_rug(alpha = 0.3, sides = "b") +
  facet_grid(`Score Type` ~ Need) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:5) +
  labs(title = "Density + Rug: Scores by Need and Group",
       x = "Score (1–5)", y = "Density", fill = LABEL_SAMPLE, color = LABEL_SAMPLE) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

fig_density
```

## 5.3 Violin + Jitter

Violin shows density shape; nested boxplot gives IQR; jitter shows individual observations.

```{r}
#| label: fig-violin
#| fig-cap: "Violin plots with nested boxplots and jittered observations for scores by need dimension and group. Width reflects density; boxes show IQR; center line shows median."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

fig_violin <- d_scores_long |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Group, y = score, fill = Group)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.5, trim = FALSE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA, fill = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.5, width = 0.15) +
  facet_grid(`Score Type` ~ Need) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = 1:5, limits = c(0.75, 5.25)) +
  labs(title = "Violin + Jitter: Scores by Need and Group",
       x = LABEL_SAMPLE, y = "Score (1–5)", fill = LABEL_SAMPLE) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "none")

fig_violin
```

## 5.4 Boxplot + Jitter

On a 1–5 scale, outlier detection via IQR ± 1.5 is not applied — values at floor/ceiling are substantively meaningful, not measurement artifacts.

```{r}
#| label: fig-box
#| fig-cap: "Boxplots with jittered individual observations for scores by need dimension and group. Whiskers extend to 1.5× IQR; floor/ceiling values are retained as substantively meaningful on a bounded scale."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

fig_box <- d_scores_long |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Group, y = score, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(`Score Type` ~ Need) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = 1:5, limits = c(0.75, 5.25)) +
  labs(title = "Boxplot + Jitter: Scores by Need and Group",
       x = LABEL_SAMPLE, y = "Score (1–5)", fill = LABEL_SAMPLE) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "none")

fig_box
```

## 5.5 Ridgeline

```{r}
#| label: fig-ridge
#| fig-cap: "Ridgeline density plots of scores by need dimension and group, facilitating direct overlap comparison between AI surrogates and human participants."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

fig_ridge <- d_scores_long |>
  ggplot(aes(x = score, y = Group, fill = Group)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.6, scale = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(`Score Type` ~ Need) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:5) +
  labs(title = "Ridgeline: Scores by Need and Group",
       x = "Score (1–5)", y = LABEL_SAMPLE, fill = LABEL_SAMPLE) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "none")

fig_ridge
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-cleanup
#| include: false

rm(d_scores_long)
```


# 6 Test assumptions

Pearson correlations
1. Continuous (interval/ratio) variables
2. Linear relationship
3. Bivariate normality 
4. Homoscedasticity 
5. Paired, independent observations
6. No influential outliers


```{r}
#| label: rq3a-setup
#| include: false

# Re-derive groups from d so any upstream filtering propagates automatically
d_grp_ai    <- d |> dplyr::filter(id_type == "AI")
d_grp_human <- d |> dplyr::filter(id_type == "HU")

# Helper: format p-values to APA style (used in sections 6 and 7)
fmt_p <- function(p) ifelse(p < .001, "< .001", sprintf("%.3f", p))

message(sprintf("RQ3a groups: N_AI = %d, N_HU = %d", nrow(d_grp_ai), nrow(d_grp_human)))
```

## 6.1 Normality Check (Q-Q Plots)

Q-Q plots compare the observed distribution to a theoretical normal distribution. Points on the diagonal indicate normality; S-curves indicate heavy tails; bends at extremes indicate floor/ceiling effects. Mild ceiling bends are expected on a 1–5 scale.

The t-test is robust to mild violations with N ≥ 30. Shapiro-Wilk results are stored in a hidden chunk for reference; with N_AI ≈ 400 the test is overpowered and a significant result may reflect trivially small deviations — Q-Q plots are the primary diagnostic.

```{r}
#| label: fig-qq-rq3a
#| fig-cap: "Normal Q-Q plots for LLM scores by need dimension and group (RQ3a normality check). Points on the diagonal indicate normality; bends at extremes reflect the bounded 1–5 scale."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-dpi: 300

fig_qq_rq3a <- d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean),
    names_to  = "variable",
    values_to = "llm_score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Need  = case_when(
      variable == "llm_aut_mean" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      variable == "llm_com_mean" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      variable == "llm_rel_mean" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    Group = c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)]
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(sample = llm_score, color = Group)) +
  stat_qq() + stat_qq_line() +
  facet_grid(Group ~ Need) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  labs(title = "Q-Q Plots: LLM Scores by Need and Group",
       x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles", color = LABEL_SAMPLE) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "none")

fig_qq_rq3a



```



```{r}
#| include: false

# Alternative qqplot
# 1. Prepare the long data (using your existing logic)
# d_long <- d |>
#   pivot_longer(
#     cols      = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean),
#     names_to  = "variable",
#     values_to = "llm_score"
#   ) |>
#   mutate(
#     Need  = case_when(
#       variable == "llm_aut_mean" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
#       variable == "llm_com_mean" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
#       variable == "llm_rel_mean" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
#     ),
#     Group = as.factor(id_type) # Ensure Group is a factor
#   )
# 
# # 2. Fit the linear model (Interaction model: Score ~ Group * Need)
# model_rq3a <- lm(llm_score ~ Group * Need, data = d_long)
# rm(d_long)
# 
# library(car)
# # Creates a QQ Plot with a 95% confidence envelope
# qqPlot(residuals(model_rq3a), distribution="norm", id=TRUE)


```

Alternative: z-scored QQ Plot

```{r}
#| label: fig-qq-rq3a-zscore
#| fig-cap: "Normal Q-Q plots (z-scored) for LLM scores by need dimension and group (RQ3a normality check). Red line is the theoretical normal reference."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

# Extract by group
aut_ai <- d$llm_aut_mean[d$id_type == "AI"]
com_ai <- d$llm_com_mean[d$id_type == "AI"]
rel_ai <- d$llm_rel_mean[d$id_type == "AI"]
aut_hu <- d$llm_aut_mean[d$id_type == "HU"]
com_hu <- d$llm_com_mean[d$id_type == "HU"]
rel_hu <- d$llm_rel_mean[d$id_type == "HU"]

# Z-score each vector
z_aut_ai <- (aut_ai - mean(aut_ai)) / sd(aut_ai)
z_com_ai <- (com_ai - mean(com_ai)) / sd(com_ai)
z_rel_ai <- (rel_ai - mean(rel_ai)) / sd(rel_ai)
z_aut_hu <- (aut_hu - mean(aut_hu)) / sd(aut_hu)
z_com_hu <- (com_hu - mean(com_hu)) / sd(com_hu)
z_rel_hu <- (rel_hu - mean(rel_hu)) / sd(rel_hu)

ylim <- c(-4, 4)
par(mfrow = c(2, 3))

qqnorm(z_aut_ai, main = paste0(LABEL_AI, ": ", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, ", z-score"), cex = 0.7, ylim = ylim)
qqline(z_aut_ai, col = "red")
qqnorm(z_com_ai, main = paste0(LABEL_AI, ": ", TABLE_LABEL_COM, ", z-score"), cex = 0.7, ylim = ylim)
qqline(z_com_ai, col = "red")
qqnorm(z_rel_ai, main = paste0(LABEL_AI, ": ", TABLE_LABEL_REL, ", z-score"), cex = 0.7, ylim = ylim)
qqline(z_rel_ai, col = "red")
qqnorm(z_aut_hu, main = paste0(LABEL_HU, ": ", TABLE_LABEL_AUT, ", z-score"), cex = 0.7, ylim = ylim)
qqline(z_aut_hu, col = "red")
qqnorm(z_com_hu, main = paste0(LABEL_HU, ": ", TABLE_LABEL_COM, ", z-score"), cex = 0.7, ylim = ylim)
qqline(z_com_hu, col = "red")
qqnorm(z_rel_hu, main = paste0(LABEL_HU, ": ", TABLE_LABEL_REL, ", z-score"), cex = 0.7, ylim = ylim)
qqline(z_rel_hu, col = "red")

rm(aut_ai, com_ai, rel_ai, aut_hu, com_hu, rel_hu,
   z_aut_ai, z_com_ai, z_rel_ai, z_aut_hu, z_com_hu, z_rel_hu)
```




```{r}
#| label: shapiro-rq3a
#| include: false

# Layer D: Shapiro-Wilk — reference only; overpowered at N ≈ 400
shapiro_rq3a <- lapply(
  setNames(c("llm_aut_mean", "llm_com_mean", "llm_rel_mean"),
           c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
  function(col) list(AI = shapiro.test(d_grp_ai[[col]]),
                     HU = shapiro.test(d_grp_human[[col]]))
)
message("Shapiro-Wilk (overpowered at N ≈ 400; use Q-Q plots as primary diagnostic):")
for (nm in names(shapiro_rq3a)) {
  message(sprintf("  %s — AI: W=%.3f p=%.4f | HU: W=%.3f p=%.4f", nm,
    shapiro_rq3a[[nm]]$AI$statistic, shapiro_rq3a[[nm]]$AI$p.value,
    shapiro_rq3a[[nm]]$HU$statistic, shapiro_rq3a[[nm]]$HU$p.value))
}
```

## 6.2 Normality Check correlations (Q-Q Plots)

For Pearson r to be valid, both variables should be approximately normally distributed within each group. With N_AI ≈ 400, mild violations are less consequential.

```{r}
#| label: fig-qq-rq3b
#| fig-cap: "Normal Q-Q plots for LLM and questionnaire scores by need dimension and group (RQ3b normality check for Pearson correlation). Rows: AI surrogates and human participants × LLM/questionnaire score type."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 10

fig_qq_rq3b <- d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
                  q_aut_mean,   q_com_mean,   q_rel_mean),
    names_to  = "variable", values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Need = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "q_aut_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      variable %in% c("llm_com_mean", "q_com_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      variable %in% c("llm_rel_mean", "q_rel_mean") ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ),
    `Score Type` = case_when(
      variable %in% c("llm_aut_mean", "llm_com_mean", "llm_rel_mean") ~ "LLM Score",
      variable %in% c("q_aut_mean",   "q_com_mean",   "q_rel_mean")   ~ "Questionnaire Score"
    ),
    Group = c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)]
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(sample = score, color = Group)) +
  stat_qq() + stat_qq_line() +
  facet_grid(Group + `Score Type` ~ Need) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  labs(title = "Q-Q Plots: LLM and Questionnaire Scores by Need and Group",
       x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles", color = LABEL_SAMPLE) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "none")

fig_qq_rq3b
```


## 6.3 Bivariate Normality Test (Mardia's Test)

Pearson r requires that the *joint* distribution of (LLM score, questionnaire score) be approximately bivariate normal — univariate normality of each variable separately (sections 6.1–6.2) is necessary but not sufficient. Mardia's test assesses bivariate normality via multivariate skewness and multivariate kurtosis; both statistics yield a p-value under the null of bivariate normality. The test is run separately for each group (AI surrogates, human participants) × need dimension combination, giving 6 tests total. With N_AI ≈ 400 the test is sensitive to small violations; Q-Q plots (section 6.2) and scatter plots (section 5) remain the primary visual diagnostic. Fisher's z-transformation of Pearson r is asymptotically valid regardless of the marginal distributions, so modest violations are not disqualifying.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-biv-normality-mardia
#| tbl-cap: "Mardia's Bivariate Normality Tests for LLM Score and Questionnaire Score Pairs by Need Dimension and Group (RQ3b Assumption Check)"
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

biv_norm_cols <- list(
  Autonomy    = list(llm = "llm_aut_mean", q = "q_aut_mean"),
  Competence  = list(llm = "llm_com_mean", q = "q_com_mean"),
  Relatedness = list(llm = "llm_rel_mean", q = "q_rel_mean")
)

biv_norm_results <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(biv_norm_cols), function(need_label) {
  llm_col <- biv_norm_cols[[need_label]]$llm
  q_col   <- biv_norm_cols[[need_label]]$q

  results <- lapply(list(AI = d_grp_ai, HU = d_grp_human), function(grp) {
    x     <- as.matrix(grp[, c(llm_col, q_col)])
    n     <- nrow(x); p <- ncol(x)
    S_inv <- solve(cov(x))
    z     <- sweep(x, 2, colMeans(x))
    M     <- z %*% S_inv %*% t(z)
    b1p   <- sum(M^3) / n^2
    b2p   <- sum(diag(M)^2) / n
    skew_stat <- n * b1p / 6
    skew_df   <- p * (p + 1) * (p + 2) / 6
    kurt_z    <- (b2p - p * (p + 2)) / sqrt(8 * p * (p + 2) / n)
    data.frame(
      Skewness_chi2 = skew_stat,
      Skewness_p    = pchisq(skew_stat, df = skew_df, lower.tail = FALSE),
      Kurtosis_z    = kurt_z,
      Kurtosis_p    = 2 * pnorm(-abs(kurt_z)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  rbind(
    cbind(Need = need_label, Group = LABEL_AI, results$AI),
    cbind(Need = need_label, Group = LABEL_HU, results$HU)
  )
}))

biv_norm_results |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    `χ²(skew)` = round(as.numeric(Skewness_chi2), 2),
    `p (skew)`  = fmt_p(as.numeric(Skewness_p)),
    `z(kurt)`   = round(as.numeric(Kurtosis_z),    2),
    `p (kurt)`  = fmt_p(as.numeric(Kurtosis_p))
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(Need, Group, `χ²(skew)`, `p (skew)`, `z(kurt)`, `p (kurt)`) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  merge_v(j = "Need") |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "Mardia’s test assesses bivariate normality of the (LLM score, questionnaire score) pair (Mardia 1970). ",
    "χ²(skew) = multivariate skewness statistic; z(kurt) = multivariate kurtosis z-score. ",
    "p < .05 indicates departure from bivariate normality. ",
    "With N ≈ 400 (AI group), the test is sensitive to minor violations. ",
    "Fisher’s z-transformation of Pearson r remains asymptotically valid under mild departures. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ", LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-biv-normality-qqplot
#| fig-cap: "Chi-square Q-Q plots of squared Mahalanobis distances for the (LLM score, questionnaire score) pair by need dimension and group (bivariate normality check). Under bivariate normality, distances follow a χ²(2) distribution and points should fall on the diagonal. Systematic upward curvature indicates heavy tails; downward curvature indicates light tails."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| warning: false

mah_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(biv_norm_cols), function(need_label) {
  llm_col <- biv_norm_cols[[need_label]]$llm
  q_col   <- biv_norm_cols[[need_label]]$q

  rbind(
    local({
      mat <- as.matrix(d_grp_ai[, c(llm_col, q_col)])
      d2  <- mahalanobis(mat, colMeans(mat), cov(mat))
      n   <- length(d2)
      data.frame(Need  = factor(need_label, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
                 Group = factor(LABEL_AI, levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU)),
                 d2 = sort(d2), chi2 = qchisq(ppoints(n), df = 2))
    }),
    local({
      mat <- as.matrix(d_grp_human[, c(llm_col, q_col)])
      d2  <- mahalanobis(mat, colMeans(mat), cov(mat))
      n   <- length(d2)
      data.frame(Need  = factor(need_label, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
                 Group = factor(LABEL_HU, levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU)),
                 d2 = sort(d2), chi2 = qchisq(ppoints(n), df = 2))
    })
  )
}))

ggplot(mah_data, aes(x = chi2, y = d2, colour = Group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.35, size = 0.9, stroke = 0) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, colour = "grey40", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6) +
  facet_grid(Group ~ Need) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  labs(x = expression(chi^2*(2)*" quantiles"), y = "Squared Mahalanobis distance") +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "none"
  )
```

## 6.4 Linearity Check (LOESS vs. OLS)

The scatter plots below show LLM score (x) against questionnaire subscale score (y) for each group × need combination. The LOESS smoother (solid) and OLS fit (dashed) are overlaid; close agreement between the two lines supports the linearity assumption required for Pearson *r*. Points are jittered to reveal density at the integer-valued scores.

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq3b-assump-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Scatter plots of LLM score (x-axis) vs. questionnaire subscale score (y-axis) by need dimension and group. Points are jittered to reveal density. Solid line = LOESS smoother; dashed line = OLS regression fit. Pearson r is shown per panel."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-dpi: 300
#| warning: false

# r labels (computed directly — rq3b_results is defined later in section 8)
r_labels_lin <- d |>
  group_by(id_type) |>
  summarise(
    aut = round(cor(llm_aut_mean, q_aut_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    com = round(cor(llm_com_mean, q_com_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    rel = round(cor(llm_rel_mean, q_rel_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(aut, com, rel), names_to = "need", values_to = "r") |>
  mutate(
    Need  = factor(case_when(
      need == "aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      need == "com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      need == "rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ), levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    Group = factor(c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)],
                   levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU)),
    label = paste0("r = ", sprintf("%.2f", r)),
    x_pos = 1.2,
    y_pos = 4.85
  )

d |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols          = c(llm_aut_mean, llm_com_mean, llm_rel_mean,
                      q_aut_mean,   q_com_mean,   q_rel_mean),
    names_to      = c("source", "need"),
    names_pattern = "(llm|q)_(aut|com|rel)_mean"
  ) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = source, values_from = value) |>
  mutate(
    Need  = factor(case_when(
      need == "aut" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      need == "com" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      need == "rel" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ), levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    Group = factor(c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)],
                   levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = llm, y = q)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.2, width = 0.15, height = 0.15, stroke = 0,
              aes(colour = Group)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", aes(colour = Group), se = FALSE,
              linewidth = 0.7, formula = y ~ x,
              method.args = list(span = 1)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", colour = "firebrick", linetype = "dashed",
              se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.7, formula = y ~ x) +
  geom_text(
    data        = r_labels_lin,
    aes(x = x_pos, y = y_pos, label = label),
    hjust       = 0, size = 3, colour = "grey30", inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_grid(Group ~ Need) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.8, 5.2), breaks = c(1, 3, 5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.8, 5.2), breaks = c(1, 3, 5)) +
  labs(x = "LLM score", y = "Questionnaire score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text             = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "none"
  )
```


# 7 RQ3a — Distributional Equivalence of LLM Scores

RQ3a tests whether AI surrogate and human participant LLM scores differ in means and variance. Welch's t-test (unequal variances) and the standard t-test (equal variances assumed) are both reported to assess sensitivity to the variance assumption. Levene's test (mean-centered) checks whether variances differ between groups. The primary effect size measure is the raw mean difference Δ = M_AI − M_human with 95% bootstrapped CI, preferred over standardized d because a high Cohen's d with low SD would itself indicate a distributional problem rather than a meaningful effect (Analytical Strategy v2026-03-24). Three tests are run (one per need dimension). These are treated as independent per-dimension questions rather than as a family requiring correction; each need dimension constitutes a separate hypothesis (RQ3a) and the study is exploratory. Accordingly, no family-wise correction is applied.

## 7.1  Welch t-Test, Standard t-Test, Levene Test, and Bootstrapped Δ

```{r}
#| label: rq3a-tests
#| include: false

set.seed(2026)
# Layer C: statistical tests per need
need_cols <- setNames(
  c("llm_aut_mean", "llm_com_mean", "llm_rel_mean"),
  c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)
)

rq3a_results <- lapply(names(need_cols), function(need_label) {
  col    <- need_cols[[need_label]]
  ai_vec <- d_grp_ai[[col]]
  hu_vec <- d_grp_human[[col]]

  t_welch    <- t.test(ai_vec, hu_vec, var.equal = FALSE)
  t_standard <- t.test(ai_vec, hu_vec, var.equal = TRUE)

  levene_data <- data.frame(score = d[[col]], id_type = d$id_type)
  levene      <- car::leveneTest(score ~ id_type, data = levene_data, center = "mean")

  # Bootstrap CIs for ΔM and ΔSD; stratified resampling preserves group sizes
  boot_data <- data.frame(score = d[[col]], id_type = d$id_type)
  boot_strata <- as.integer(factor(d$id_type))

  boot_fn_delta <- function(data, i) {
    db <- data[i, ]
    mean(db$score[db$id_type == "AI"]) - mean(db$score[db$id_type == "HU"])
  }
  boot_obj <- boot::boot(boot_data, boot_fn_delta, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, strata = boot_strata)
  ci <- boot::boot.ci(boot_obj, type = "perc")

  boot_fn_delta_sd <- function(data, i) {
    db <- data[i, ]
    sd(db$score[db$id_type == "AI"]) - sd(db$score[db$id_type == "HU"])
  }
  boot_obj_sd <- boot::boot(boot_data, boot_fn_delta_sd, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, strata = boot_strata)
  ci_sd <- boot::boot.ci(boot_obj_sd, type = "perc")

  # Flag if Welch and standard t diverge — Levene p < .05 explains the divergence
  if (abs(t_welch$statistic - t_standard$statistic) > 0.5) {
    message(sprintf(
      "Note (%s): Welch t (%.2f) and standard t (%.2f) diverge. Levene p = %.3f.",
      need_label, t_welch$statistic, t_standard$statistic, levene[1, "Pr(>F)"]))
  }

  data.frame(
    need       = need_label,
    m_ai       = mean(ai_vec),   sd_ai    = sd(ai_vec),
    m_hu       = mean(hu_vec),   sd_hu    = sd(hu_vec),
    delta      = mean(ai_vec) - mean(hu_vec),
    ci_lo      = ci$percent[4],    ci_hi    = ci$percent[5],
    delta_sd   = sd(ai_vec) - sd(hu_vec),
    sd_ci_lo   = ci_sd$percent[4], sd_ci_hi = ci_sd$percent[5],
    welch_t    = as.numeric(t_welch$statistic),
    welch_df   = as.numeric(t_welch$parameter),
    welch_p    = t_welch$p.value,
    std_t      = as.numeric(t_standard$statistic),
    std_df     = as.numeric(t_standard$parameter),
    std_p      = t_standard$p.value,
    levene_f   = as.numeric(levene[1, "F value"]),
    levene_df1 = as.integer(levene[1, "Df"]),
    levene_df2 = as.integer(levene[2, "Df"]),
    levene_p   = as.numeric(levene[1, "Pr(>F)"]),
    sd_ratio   = sd(ai_vec) / sd(hu_vec),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

rq3a_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(rq3a_results)
```



```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq3a-desc-groupmeans
#| tbl-cap: "Mean and SD of LLM Scores by Need Dimension and Group (RQ3a: Distributional Equivalence)"
#| echo: false

tbl_rq3a_desc_groupmeans <- rq3a_df |>
  transmute(
    Need        = need,
    `M (AI)`    = round(m_ai,              2),
    `SD (AI)`   = round(sd_ai,             2),
    `M (Human)` = round(m_hu,              2),
    `SD (Human)`= round(sd_hu,             2),
    `ΔM`        = round(m_ai  - m_hu,      2),
    `ΔSD`       = round(sd_ai - sd_hu,     2)
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "LLM scores on a 1–5 scale. ",
    "ΔM = M(AI) − M(Human); ΔSD = SD(AI) − SD(Human). ",
    "Positive ΔSD indicates AI surrogates have higher variance. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ", LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))

tbl_rq3a_desc_groupmeans
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq3a-welch-levene
#| tbl-cap: "Mean and Variance Differences in LLM Scores Between AI Surrogates and Human Participants with Welch's t-Test and Levene's Variance Test (RQ3a)"
#| echo: false

tbl_rq3a_welch_levene <- rq3a_df |>
  transmute(
    Need                = need,
    `ΔM [95% CI]`       = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", delta, ci_lo, ci_hi),
    `ΔSD [95% CI]`      = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", delta_sd, sd_ci_lo, sd_ci_hi),
    `Welch t(df)`       = sprintf("%.2f (%.1f)", welch_t, welch_df),
    `p (Welch)`         = fmt_p(welch_p),
    `Standard t(df)`    = sprintf("%.2f (%.1f)", std_t, std_df),
    `p (std.)`          = fmt_p(std_p),
    `Levene F(df₁,df₂)` = sprintf("%.2f(%d,%d)", levene_f, levene_df1, levene_df2),
    `p (Levene)`        = fmt_p(levene_p)
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "ΔM = M_AI − M_Human; ΔSD = SD_AI − SD_Human. ",
    "CIs: 95% bootstrap percentile (R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, ", stratified by group). ",
    "Welch's t-test does not assume equal variances; both versions reported for sensitivity. ",
    "Levene's test (mean-centered): p < .05 indicates unequal variances. ",
    "If Welch and standard t diverge substantially, Levene p < .05 indicates variance drives the difference."
  ))

tbl_rq3a_welch_levene
```
## 7.2 Means and SDs bootstrapped

```{r}
#| label: rq3a-boot-ci
#| include: false

set.seed(2026)
# Bootstrap CIs for group-level M and SD per need (separate from the delta bootstrap above)
boot_rq3a_list <- lapply(names(need_cols), function(need_label) {
  col    <- need_cols[[need_label]]
  ai_vec <- d_grp_ai[[col]]
  hu_vec <- d_grp_human[[col]]

  ci_m_ai  <- boot::boot.ci(boot::boot(ai_vec, function(x, i) mean(x[i]), R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES), type = "perc")
  ci_m_hu  <- boot::boot.ci(boot::boot(hu_vec, function(x, i) mean(x[i]), R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES), type = "perc")
  ci_sd_ai <- boot::boot.ci(boot::boot(ai_vec, function(x, i)   sd(x[i]), R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES), type = "perc")
  ci_sd_hu <- boot::boot.ci(boot::boot(hu_vec, function(x, i)   sd(x[i]), R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES), type = "perc")

  data.frame(
    need      = need_label,
    group     = c(LABEL_AI,            LABEL_HU),
    m         = c(mean(ai_vec),         mean(hu_vec)),
    m_ci_lo   = c(ci_m_ai$percent[4],   ci_m_hu$percent[4]),
    m_ci_hi   = c(ci_m_ai$percent[5],   ci_m_hu$percent[5]),
    sd        = c(sd(ai_vec),            sd(hu_vec)),
    sd_ci_lo  = c(ci_sd_ai$percent[4],  ci_sd_hu$percent[4]),
    sd_ci_hi  = c(ci_sd_ai$percent[5],  ci_sd_hu$percent[5]),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

boot_rq3a_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(boot_rq3a_list) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need  = factor(need,  levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    group = factor(group, levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))
  )
```


```{r}
#| label: fig-rq3a-mean-bars
#| fig-cap: "Mean LLM scores by need dimension and group (RQ3a). Bars = observed mean; error bars = ±1 SD."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

ggplot(boot_rq3a_df, aes(x = need, y = m, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), width = 0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = m - sd, ymax = m + sd),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width = 0.25, linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1, 5)) +
  labs(
    title    = "RQ3a: Mean LLM Scores by Need and Group",
    subtitle = "Error bars = ±1 SD",
    x        = "Need Dimension",
    y        = "Mean LLM Score",
    fill     = LABEL_SAMPLE
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text            = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```


### Means and SDs — shaded CIs

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq3a-mean-ci
#| fig-cap: "Mean LLM scores by need dimension and group (RQ3a). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs. Yellow bracket = Δ mean (AI − Human)."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 500

DODGE_W <- 0.1
TICK_W  <- 0.10

group_colors_rq3a <- c(
  setNames(COL_AI_LINE, LABEL_AI),
  setNames(COL_HU_LINE, LABEL_HU)
)

fig_rq3a_mean_data <- boot_rq3a_df |>
  dplyr::mutate(x = as.numeric(need) + ifelse(group == LABEL_HU, -DODGE_W, DODGE_W))

bracket_m <- boot_rq3a_df |>
  dplyr::select(need, group, m) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = m) |>
  dplyr::rename(y_ai = dplyr::all_of(LABEL_AI), y_hu = dplyr::all_of(LABEL_HU)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(x_arm = as.numeric(need))

fig_rq3a_mean_ci <- ggplot(fig_rq3a_mean_data,
    aes(x = x, y = m, color = group, fill = group, group = group)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = m_ci_lo, ymax = m_ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8) +
  geom_segment(data = bracket_m,
    aes(x = x_arm, xend = x_arm, y = y_hu, yend = y_ai),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(data = bracket_m,
    aes(x = x_arm - TICK_W, xend = x_arm + TICK_W, y = y_ai, yend = y_ai),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(data = bracket_m,
    aes(x = x_arm - TICK_W, xend = x_arm + TICK_W, y = y_hu, yend = y_hu),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks   = seq_along(levels(boot_rq3a_df$need)),
    labels   = levels(boot_rq3a_df$need),
    limits   = c(0.6, 3.6),
    position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors_rq3a, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = group_colors_rq3a, name = NULL) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE), fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(1, 5)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean LLM Score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x.top    = element_text(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.2, 0.15),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq3a_mean_ci
```

*Note.* Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). AI surrogates N = `r nrow(d_grp_ai)`; Humans N = `r nrow(d_grp_human)`. Yellow bracket = Δ mean (AI − Human).

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq3a-sd-ci
#| fig-cap: "SD of LLM scores by need dimension and group (RQ3a). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs. Yellow bracket = Δ SD (AI − Human)."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 500

bracket_sd <- boot_rq3a_df |>
  dplyr::select(need, group, sd) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = sd) |>
  dplyr::rename(y_ai = dplyr::all_of(LABEL_AI), y_hu = dplyr::all_of(LABEL_HU)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(x_arm = as.numeric(need))

fig_rq3a_sd_ci <- ggplot(
    boot_rq3a_df |> dplyr::mutate(x = as.numeric(need) + ifelse(group == LABEL_HU, -DODGE_W, DODGE_W)),
    aes(x = x, y = sd, color = group, fill = group, group = group)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = sd_ci_lo, ymax = sd_ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8) +
  geom_segment(data = bracket_sd,
    aes(x = x_arm, xend = x_arm, y = y_hu, yend = y_ai),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(data = bracket_sd,
    aes(x = x_arm - TICK_W, xend = x_arm + TICK_W, y = y_ai, yend = y_ai),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(data = bracket_sd,
    aes(x = x_arm - TICK_W, xend = x_arm + TICK_W, y = y_hu, yend = y_hu),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks   = seq_along(levels(boot_rq3a_df$need)),
    labels   = levels(boot_rq3a_df$need),
    limits   = c(0.6, 3.6),
    position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.15, 0.8)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors_rq3a, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = group_colors_rq3a, name = NULL) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE), fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +

  labs(x = NULL, y = "SD of LLM Score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x.top    = element_text(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.25, 0.15),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq3a_sd_ci


```

*Note.* Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). AI surrogates N = `r nrow(d_grp_ai)`; Humans N = `r nrow(d_grp_human)`. Yellow bracket = Δ SD (AI − Human).


# 8 RQ3b — Difference in LLM–Questionnaire Correlations

RQ3b tests whether the LLM–questionnaire correlation differs between AI surrogates and human participants, per need. Pearson r is computed per group and compared using Fisher's z-difference test for independent samples (`psych::r.test`). Spearman ρ is computed as a robustness check; both are reported. The primary metric is Δr = r_AI − r_human with 95% bootstrapped CI. With N_AI ≈ 400 vs. N_HU ≈ 120, p-values are sensitive to small differences and are secondary. 

## 8.1 Preparation


### 8.1.1 Compute Pearson r and Spearman ρ

```{r}
#| label: rq3b-correlations
#| include: false

set.seed(2026)
# Layer C: correlations + per-group bootstrap CIs per need
q_cols <- setNames(
  c("q_aut_mean", "q_com_mean", "q_rel_mean"),
  c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)
)


boot_fn_r <- function(data, i) cor(data$llm[i], data$q[i], method = "pearson")

rq3b_results <- lapply(names(need_cols), function(need_label) {
  llm_col <- need_cols[[need_label]]
  q_col   <- q_cols[[need_label]]

  r_ai   <- cor(d_grp_ai[[llm_col]],    d_grp_ai[[q_col]],    method = "pearson")
  r_hu   <- cor(d_grp_human[[llm_col]], d_grp_human[[q_col]], method = "pearson")
  rho_ai <- cor(d_grp_ai[[llm_col]],    d_grp_ai[[q_col]],    method = "spearman")
  rho_hu <- cor(d_grp_human[[llm_col]], d_grp_human[[q_col]], method = "spearman")

  boot_ai <- boot::boot(
    data.frame(llm = d_grp_ai[[llm_col]], q = d_grp_ai[[q_col]]),
    boot_fn_r, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
  ci_ai <- boot::boot.ci(boot_ai, type = "perc")

  boot_hu <- boot::boot(
    data.frame(llm = d_grp_human[[llm_col]], q = d_grp_human[[q_col]]),
    boot_fn_r, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
  ci_hu <- boot::boot.ci(boot_hu, type = "perc")

  list(
    need       = need_label,
    r_ai       = r_ai,       r_hu       = r_hu,
    rho_ai     = rho_ai,     rho_hu     = rho_hu,
    r_ai_ci_lo = ci_ai$percent[4], r_ai_ci_hi = ci_ai$percent[5],
    r_hu_ci_lo = ci_hu$percent[4], r_hu_ci_hi = ci_hu$percent[5],
    boot_r_ai  = boot_ai$t[, 1],   # raw bootstrap r values for fig_fisher_transform
    boot_r_hu  = boot_hu$t[, 1]
  )
})
```

### 8.1.2 Fisher z-Difference Test

Fisher's z-difference test (`psych::r.test`) compares Pearson r values across two independent groups. The function applies the Fisher r-to-z transformation internally (z = 0.5 × ln((1+r)/(1-r))). This transformation is required because raw Pearson r values are not normally distributed — they are bounded [−1, 1] and right-skewed near ±1; the transformation normalizes the distribution, enabling a valid z-test. The parameter names `r12` and `r34` are `psych`'s internal naming convention for "group 1 correlation" and "group 2 correlation" — not meaningful variable names.

```{r}
#| label: rq3b-fisher
#| include: false

set.seed(2026)
# Layer C: Fisher z-difference + bootstrap CI for Δr per need
boot_fn_delta_r <- function(data, i) {
  db  <- data[i, ]
  r_a <- cor(db$llm[db$id_type == "AI"], db$q[db$id_type == "AI"], method = "pearson")
```
