```{r}
  r_h <- cor(db$llm[db$id_type == "HU"], db$q[db$id_type == "HU"], method = "pearson")
  r_a - r_h
}

rq3b_fisher_results <- lapply(rq3b_results, function(res) {
  llm_col <- need_cols[[res$need]]
  q_col   <- q_cols[[res$need]]

  # psych::r.test applies Fisher r-to-z internally
  fisher_test <- psych::r.test(
    n   = nrow(d_grp_ai),     # N for AI group
    r12 = res$r_ai,            # Pearson r, AI group (psych param naming convention)
    n2  = nrow(d_grp_human),  # N for human group
    r34 = res$r_hu             # Pearson r, human group (psych param naming convention)
  )

  # Bootstrap CI for Δr (stratified resampling preserves group sizes)
  boot_delta <- boot::boot(
    data.frame(llm = d[[llm_col]], q = d[[q_col]], id_type = d$id_type),
    boot_fn_delta_r, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES,
    strata = as.integer(factor(d$id_type))
  )
  ci_delta <- boot::boot.ci(boot_delta, type = "perc")

  data.frame(
    need        = res$need,
    delta_r     = res$r_ai - res$r_hu,
    delta_ci_lo = ci_delta$percent[4],
    delta_ci_hi = ci_delta$percent[5],
    delta_se    = sd(boot_delta$t[, 1]),
    z           = fisher_test$z,
    p           = fisher_test$p,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

rq3b_fisher_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(rq3b_fisher_results)
```


## 8.2 RQ3b Results Table

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff
#| tbl-cap: "Pearson Correlations Between LLM and Questionnaire Scores by Group and Fisher z-Difference Test (RQ3b)"
#| echo: false

tbl_rq3b_fisher_zdiff <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(rq3b_results, function(x) {
  data.frame(
    need  = x$need,
    r_hu  = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", x$r_hu, x$r_hu_ci_lo, x$r_hu_ci_hi),
    r_ai  = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", x$r_ai, x$r_ai_ci_lo, x$r_ai_ci_hi),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    rq3b_fisher_df |>
      dplyr::transmute(
        need,
        delta = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", delta_r, delta_ci_lo, delta_ci_hi),
        Z     = round(z, 2),
        p     = fmt_p(p)
      ),
    by = "need"
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    need  = LABEL_NEEDS,
    r_hu  = paste0("r [", CI_LABEL, "]"),
    r_ai  = paste0("r [", CI_LABEL, "]"),
    delta = paste0("\u0394r [", CI_LABEL, "]"),
    Z     = "Z",
    p     = "p"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI, "Difference", "", ""),
    colwidths = c(1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "r = Pearson correlation between LLM score (mean of 3 repeats) and questionnaire score. ",
    "CIs: 95% bootstrap percentile (R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "). ",
    "\u0394r = r(AI surrogates) \u2212 r(Humans); CI for \u0394r bootstrapped stratified by group. ",
    "Z and p from Fisher\u2019s z-difference test for independent correlations (psych::r.test). ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ", LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))

tbl_rq3b_fisher_zdiff
```

### 8.2.1 Correlation Estimates with SE

The bootstrap percentile method takes the 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles of the bootstrap distribution directly, rather than constructing a symmetric interval around the estimate using ±1.96 × SE. These two approaches only agree when the bootstrap distribution is perfectly normal and symmetric.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq3b-se
#| tbl-cap: "Pearson Correlations Between LLM and Questionnaire Scores with Bootstrapped SE by Group (RQ3b)"
#| echo: false

tbl_rq3b_se <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(rq3b_results, function(x) {
  data.frame(
    need  = x$need,
    r_hu  = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", x$r_hu, x$r_hu_ci_lo, x$r_hu_ci_hi),
    se_hu = sprintf("%.3f", sd(x$boot_r_hu)),
    r_ai  = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", x$r_ai, x$r_ai_ci_lo, x$r_ai_ci_hi),
    se_ai = sprintf("%.3f", sd(x$boot_r_ai)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    rq3b_fisher_df |>
      dplyr::transmute(
        need,
        delta    = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", delta_r, delta_ci_lo, delta_ci_hi),
        se_delta = sprintf("%.3f", delta_se)
      ),
    by = "need"
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    need     = LABEL_NEEDS,
    r_hu     = paste0("r [", CI_LABEL, "]"),
    se_hu    = "SE",
    r_ai     = paste0("r [", CI_LABEL, "]"),
    se_ai    = "SE",
    delta    = paste0("Δr [", CI_LABEL, "]"),
    se_delta = "SE"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI, "Difference"),
    colwidths = c(1L, 2L, 2L, 2L)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "r = Pearson correlation between LLM score (mean of 3 repeats) and questionnaire score. ",
    "SE = standard deviation of the bootstrap distribution (R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "). ",
    "CIs: 95% bootstrap percentile. ",
    "Δr = r(AI surrogates) − r(Humans); SE for Δr bootstrapped stratified by group. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ", LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))

tbl_rq3b_se
```


The SEs look modest in absolute terms because correlations are bounded in [−1, +1]. However, they are not small relative to the estimates — they're actually large relative to the effect sizes, which are themselves near zero. Since r is so small, r is approximately normally distributed — that's why the standard formula ±1.96 results in the same CI as the bootstrapped percentiles.

### 8.3 Fisher Transformation Visualization

The plot below shows the bootstrap distribution of raw Pearson r (left column) and Fisher z-transformed values (right column) for each need dimension (rows) and group. Raw r is bounded to [−1, 1] and may be skewed when correlations are moderate to strong; the Fisher z-transformation yields approximately symmetric distributions, which is the assumption required by the z-difference test. For correlations near zero, this approximates to z ≈ r — the two are nearly identical numerically.

```{r}
#| label: fig-fisher-transform
#| fig-cap: "Bootstrap distributions of raw Pearson r (left) and Fisher z-transformed values (right) by need dimension and group. The z-transformation yields approximately symmetric distributions, satisfying the normality assumption required for the z-test."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8

fisher_viz_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(rq3b_results, function(x) {
  rbind(
    data.frame(Need = x$need, Group = LABEL_AI, raw_r = x$boot_r_ai),
    data.frame(Need = x$need, Group = LABEL_HU, raw_r = x$boot_r_hu)
  )
})) |>
  mutate(
    z_transformed = psych::fisherz(raw_r),
    Need  = factor(Need,  levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    Group = factor(Group, levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))
  )

fig_fisher_transform <- fisher_viz_data |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(raw_r, z_transformed),
                      names_to = "Transformation", values_to = "value") |>
  mutate(Transformation = factor(Transformation,
    levels = c("raw_r", "z_transformed"),
    labels = c("Raw Pearson r", "Fisher z-transformed"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = Group)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = 0.6, position = "identity") +
  facet_grid(Need ~ Transformation, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  labs(
    title    = "Bootstrap Distribution: Raw Pearson r vs. Fisher z-Transformed",
    subtitle = paste0("R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, " bootstrap resamples per group × need"),
    x = "Value", y = "Frequency", fill = LABEL_SAMPLE,
    caption  = paste0(
      "Left: raw r — bounded [−1,1], right-skewed near ±1. ",
      "Right: Fisher z = 0.5 × ln((1+r)/(1−r)) — approximately symmetric. ",
      "Symmetry enables the z-test in psych::r.test()."
    )
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

fig_fisher_transform
```

### 8.4 Pearson vs Spearman check

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq3b-pearson-spearman
#| tbl-cap: "Pearson r and Spearman ρ Between LLM Scores and Questionnaire Scores by Need Dimension and Group (RQ3b)"
#| echo: false

tbl_rq3b_pearson_spearman <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(rq3b_results, function(x) {
  data.frame(
    Need                 = x$need,
    `r (AI) [95% CI]`    = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", x$r_ai, x$r_ai_ci_lo, x$r_ai_ci_hi),
    `ρ (AI)`             = round(x$rho_ai, 2),
    `r (Human) [95% CI]` = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", x$r_hu, x$r_hu_ci_lo, x$r_hu_ci_hi),
    `ρ (Human)`          = round(x$rho_hu, 2),
    check.names = FALSE
  )
})) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "r = Pearson correlation; ρ = Spearman (robustness check). ",
    "CIs: 95% bootstrap percentile (R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "). ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ", LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), ". "
  ))

tbl_rq3b_pearson_spearman
```

## 8.5 Visualisation

### 8.5.1 Correlations per group


```{r}
#| label: fig-rq3b-corr-CI
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Pearson r between LLM scores and questionnaire scores by need dimension and group (RQ3b). Shaded bands and error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs."
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 500

# geom_ribbon requires a continuous x; manually apply dodge offset so ribbons
# don't overlap (position_dodge has no effect on geom_ribbon).
DODGE_W <- 0.1

fig_rq3b_corr_ci_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(rq3b_results, function(x) {
  rbind(
    data.frame(need = x$need, group = LABEL_AI, r = x$r_ai,
               ci_lo = x$r_ai_ci_lo, ci_hi = x$r_ai_ci_hi, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
    data.frame(need = x$need, group = LABEL_HU, r = x$r_hu,
               ci_lo = x$r_hu_ci_lo, ci_hi = x$r_hu_ci_hi, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  )
})) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need  = factor(need,  levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    group = factor(group, levels = c(LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI)),
    x     = as.numeric(need) + ifelse(group == LABEL_HU, -DODGE_W, DODGE_W)
  )

rq3b_group_colors_ci <- c(
  setNames(COL_AI_SHADES[1], LABEL_AI),
  setNames(COL_HU_SHADES[1], LABEL_HU)
)

BRACKET_X  <- 0     # arm centered between the two dodged points (DODGE_W = 0.1)
TICK_W     <- 0.10  # horizontal tick half-width

bracket_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(rq3b_results, function(x) {
  need_num <- which(c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL) == x$need)
  data.frame(
    need_num = need_num,
    r_ai     = x$r_ai,
    r_hu     = x$r_hu,
    delta_r  = x$r_ai - x$r_hu,
    x_arm    = need_num + BRACKET_X,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

fig_rq3b_corr_CI <- ggplot(fig_rq3b_corr_ci_data,
    aes(x = x, y = r, color = group, fill = group, group = group)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5, color = "grey60") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8) +
  # bracket: vertical arm
  geom_segment(data = bracket_data,
    aes(x = x_arm, xend = x_arm, y = r_hu, yend = r_ai),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  # bracket: top tick
  geom_segment(data = bracket_data,
    aes(x = x_arm - TICK_W, xend = x_arm + TICK_W, y = r_ai, yend = r_ai),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  # bracket: bottom tick
  geom_segment(data = bracket_data,
    aes(x = x_arm - TICK_W, xend = x_arm + TICK_W, y = r_hu, yend = r_hu),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks   = seq_along(levels(fig_rq3b_corr_ci_data$need)),
    labels   = levels(fig_rq3b_corr_ci_data$need),
    limits   = c(0.6, 3.6),
    position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = rq3b_group_colors_ci, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = rq3b_group_colors_ci, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Pearson r") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x.top    = element_text(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.18, 0.15),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq3b_corr_CI

```

*Note.* Shaded bands and error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). AI surrogates N = `r nrow(d_grp_ai)`; Humans N = `r nrow(d_grp_human)`.

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq3b-corr-CI-rect
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Pearson r between LLM scores and questionnaire scores by need dimension and group (RQ3b). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs. Shaded rectangles = difference between point estimates (Δr)."
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 500

DODGE_W    <- 0.1
RECT_HALF_W <- 0.04  # narrower than dodge offset; avoids overlap with CI bars

fig_rq3b_corr_ci_rect_data <- fig_rq3b_corr_ci_data  # reuse data built above

fig_rq3b_corr_CI_rect <- ggplot(fig_rq3b_corr_ci_rect_data,
    aes(x = x, y = r, color = group, fill = group, group = group)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5, color = "grey60") +
  # shaded rectangle spanning the gap between the two point estimates
  geom_rect(data = bracket_data,
    aes(xmin = x_arm - RECT_HALF_W, xmax = x_arm + RECT_HALF_W,
        ymin = pmin(r_hu, r_ai),     ymax = pmax(r_hu, r_ai)),
    fill = COL_DIFF, alpha = 0.45, color = NA,
    inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks   = seq_along(levels(fig_rq3b_corr_ci_rect_data$need)),
    labels   = levels(fig_rq3b_corr_ci_rect_data$need),
    limits   = c(0.6, 3.6),
    position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = rq3b_group_colors_ci, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = rq3b_group_colors_ci, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Pearson r") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x.top    = element_text(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position      = c(0.18, 0.15),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank(),
    legend.title         = element_text(face = "plain")
  )

fig_rq3b_corr_CI_rect

```

*Note.* Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`). Shaded rectangles = difference between point estimates (Δr = r_AI − r_Human). AI surrogates N = `r nrow(d_grp_ai)`; Humans N = `r nrow(d_grp_human)`.

### 8.5.2 Δr from Fisher's z-difference test

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq3b-delta
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Δr (r_AI − r_Human) per need dimension with 95% bootstrap CIs (RQ3b). Dashed line = 0 (no group difference). Points above zero indicate stronger LLM–questionnaire correspondence in AI surrogates than humans."
#| fig-width: 5
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

fig_rq3b_delta_data <- rq3b_fisher_df |>
  dplyr::mutate(need = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)))

fig_rq3b_delta <- ggplot(fig_rq3b_delta_data,
    aes(x = need, y = delta_r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4, color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = delta_ci_lo, ymax = delta_ci_hi),
                width = 0, linewidth = 0.8, color = COL_DIFF) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8,
             color = COL_DIFF) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = expression(Delta*italic(r) ~ "(AI \u2212 Human)")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x.top    = element_text(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12))
  )

fig_rq3b_delta

```

*Note.* Δr = r(AI surrogates) − r(Humans). Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs for Δr (R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`, stratified by group). Z and p from Fisher's z-difference test; see @tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff.


# 9 Sensitivity: Equal-Width Binning of LLM Scores

LLM score means are continuous averages of three repeated scorings on a 1–5 integer scale. This section applies equal-width binning by rounding each mean to the nearest integer ($3.1 \to 3$, $3.6 \to 4$), collapsing the continuous distribution back onto the five integer bins. Correlations between binned LLM scores and questionnaire scores are then recomputed for both groups and compared to the unrounded results in @tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff. All code is self-contained and does not modify any upstream objects.

## 9.1 Histogram of Rounded LLM Scores

```{r}
#| label: fig-hist-llm-rounded
#| fig-cap: "Frequency distributions of LLM scores after rounding to the nearest integer (equal-width binning, bin width = 1), by need dimension and sample group. Vertical lines indicate group means of the rounded scores. Compare with the unrounded distributions in @fig-hist."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-dpi: 300

# Derived locally — does not overwrite d or any upstream object
d_rounded <- d |>
  mutate(
    llm_aut_round = round(llm_aut_mean),
    llm_com_round = round(llm_com_mean),
    llm_rel_round = round(llm_rel_mean)
  )

d_rounded_long <- d_rounded |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(llm_aut_round, llm_com_round, llm_rel_round),
    names_to  = "variable",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Need  = factor(case_when(
      variable == "llm_aut_round" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      variable == "llm_com_round" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      variable == "llm_rel_round" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ), levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    Group = factor(c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)],
                   levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))
  )

means_rounded <- d_rounded_long |>
  group_by(Group, Need) |>
  summarise(M = mean(score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

d_rounded_long |>
  ggplot(aes(x = score, fill = Group)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 0.5, position = "dodge",
                 alpha = 0.85, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(data = means_rounded,
             aes(xintercept = M, color = Group, linetype = Group),
             linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Need, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:5, limits = c(0.5, 5.5)) +
  labs(x = "Rounded LLM Score (1–5)", y = "Count",
       fill = LABEL_SAMPLE, color = "Group (mean)", linetype = "Group (mean)") +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

```

## 9.2 Correlations with Rounded LLM Scores

```{r}
#| label: tbl-corr-rounded
#| tbl-cap: "Pearson Correlations Between Rounded LLM Scores and Questionnaire Scores by Need Dimension and Group (Equal-Width Binning Sensitivity Check)"
#| echo: false

corr_rounded_ai <- d_rounded |> dplyr::filter(id_type == "AI") |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    !!TABLE_LABEL_AUT := round(cor(llm_aut_round, q_aut_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_COM := round(cor(llm_com_round, q_com_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_REL := round(cor(llm_rel_round, q_rel_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2)
  )

corr_rounded_hu <- d_rounded |> dplyr::filter(id_type == "HU") |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    !!TABLE_LABEL_AUT := round(cor(llm_aut_round, q_aut_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_COM := round(cor(llm_com_round, q_com_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_REL := round(cor(llm_rel_round, q_rel_mean, use = "complete.obs"), 2)
  )

# Pull unrounded r values from rq3b_results for comparison
r_unrounded_ai <- sapply(rq3b_results, `[[`, "r_ai")
r_unrounded_hu <- sapply(rq3b_results, `[[`, "r_hu")

tibble::tibble(
  Need         = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
  !!LABEL_HU  := c(corr_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]],
                    corr_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]],
                    corr_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]]),
  `Δr (HU)`   = round(c(corr_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]],
                          corr_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]],
                          corr_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]]) - r_unrounded_hu, 2),
  !!LABEL_AI  := c(corr_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]],
                    corr_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]],
                    corr_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]]),
  `Δr (AI)`   = round(c(corr_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]],
                          corr_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]],
                          corr_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]]) - r_unrounded_ai, 2)
) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    `Δr (HU)` = "Δr",
    `Δr (AI)` = "Δr"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI),
    colwidths = c(1L, 2L, 2L)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "LLM scores rounded to the nearest integer before correlation (e.g., 3.1 → 3, 3.6 → 4). ",
    "Questionnaire scores are unmodified means. ",
    "Δr = r(rounded) − r(unrounded); reference values from @tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ",
    LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))
```


## 9.3 Histogram of Rounded Questionnaire Scores

```{r}
#| label: fig-hist-q-rounded
#| fig-cap: "Frequency distributions of questionnaire scores after rounding composite means to the nearest integer (equal-width binning, bin width = 1), by need dimension and sample group. Vertical lines indicate group means of the rounded scores. Compare with the unrounded distributions in @fig-hist."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 5

d_q_rounded <- d |>
  mutate(
    llm_aut_round = round(llm_aut_mean),
    llm_com_round = round(llm_com_mean),
    llm_rel_round = round(llm_rel_mean),
    q_aut_round   = round(q_aut_mean),
    q_com_round   = round(q_com_mean),
    q_rel_round   = round(q_rel_mean)
  )

d_q_rounded_long <- d_q_rounded |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(q_aut_round, q_com_round, q_rel_round),
    names_to  = "variable",
    values_to = "score"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Need  = factor(case_when(
      variable == "q_aut_round" ~ TABLE_LABEL_AUT,
      variable == "q_com_round" ~ TABLE_LABEL_COM,
      variable == "q_rel_round" ~ TABLE_LABEL_REL
    ), levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)),
    Group = factor(c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)],
                   levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))
  )

means_q_rounded <- d_q_rounded_long |>
  group_by(Group, Need) |>
  summarise(M = mean(score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

d_q_rounded_long |>
  ggplot(aes(x = score, fill = Group)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 0.5, position = "dodge",
                 alpha = 0.85, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(data = means_q_rounded,
             aes(xintercept = M, color = Group, linetype = Group),
             linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Need, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:5, limits = c(0.5, 5.5)) +
  labs(x = "Rounded Questionnaire Score (1–5)", y = "Count",
       fill = LABEL_SAMPLE, color = "Group (mean)", linetype = "Group (mean)") +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

```

## 9.4 Correlations: Rounded LLM Scores × Rounded Questionnaire Scores

```{r}
#| label: tbl-corr-q-rounded
#| tbl-cap: "Pearson Correlations Between Rounded LLM Scores and Rounded Questionnaire Scores by Need Dimension and Group (Both Variables Binned — Equal-Width Sensitivity Check)"
#| echo: false

corr_q_rounded_ai <- d_q_rounded |> dplyr::filter(id_type == "AI") |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    !!TABLE_LABEL_AUT := round(cor(llm_aut_round, q_aut_round, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_COM := round(cor(llm_com_round, q_com_round, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_REL := round(cor(llm_rel_round, q_rel_round, use = "complete.obs"), 2)
  )

corr_q_rounded_hu <- d_q_rounded |> dplyr::filter(id_type == "HU") |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    !!TABLE_LABEL_AUT := round(cor(llm_aut_round, q_aut_round, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_COM := round(cor(llm_com_round, q_com_round, use = "complete.obs"), 2),
    !!TABLE_LABEL_REL := round(cor(llm_rel_round, q_rel_round, use = "complete.obs"), 2)
  )

r_unrounded_ai <- sapply(rq3b_results, `[[`, "r_ai")
r_unrounded_hu <- sapply(rq3b_results, `[[`, "r_hu")

tibble::tibble(
  Need         = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
  !!LABEL_HU  := c(corr_q_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]],
                    corr_q_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]],
                    corr_q_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]]),
  `Δr (HU)`   = round(c(corr_q_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]],
                          corr_q_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]],
                          corr_q_rounded_hu[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]]) - r_unrounded_hu, 2),
  !!LABEL_AI  := c(corr_q_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]],
                    corr_q_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]],
                    corr_q_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]]),
  `Δr (AI)`   = round(c(corr_q_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_AUT]],
                          corr_q_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_COM]],
                          corr_q_rounded_ai[[TABLE_LABEL_REL]]) - r_unrounded_ai, 2)
) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    `Δr (HU)` = "Δr",
    `Δr (AI)` = "Δr"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI),
    colwidths = c(1L, 2L, 2L)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "Both LLM scores and questionnaire scores rounded to the nearest integer before correlation (e.g., 3.1 → 3, 3.6 → 4). ",
    "Δr = r(both rounded) − r(unrounded); reference values from @tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ",
    LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))
```

## 9.5 Competence Cross-Tabulation (Rounded LLM × Rounded Questionnaire)

```{r}
#| label: tbl-comp-crosstab-ai
#| tbl-cap: "Cross-Tabulation of Rounded LLM Competence and Rounded Questionnaire Competence — AI Surrogates"
#| echo: false

ct_ai_df <- with(
  dplyr::filter(d_q_rounded, id_type == "AI"),
  table(
    `LLM Competence` = factor(llm_com_round, levels = 1:5),
    `Q Competence`   = factor(q_com_round,   levels = 1:5)
  )
) |> as.data.frame.matrix()
ct_ai_df <- cbind(LLM = rownames(ct_ai_df), ct_ai_df)

ct_ai_df |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(LLM = "LLM Competence (rounded)") |>
  add_header_row(values = c("", "Questionnaire Competence (rounded)"), colwidths = c(1L, 5L)) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "Rows = rounded LLM Competence (1–5); columns = rounded questionnaire Competence (1–5). ",
    "Cell entries are counts. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", sum(d_q_rounded$id_type == "AI"), "."
  ))
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-comp-crosstab-hu
#| tbl-cap: "Cross-Tabulation of Rounded LLM Competence and Rounded Questionnaire Competence — Human Participants"
#| echo: false

ct_hu_df <- with(
  dplyr::filter(d_q_rounded, id_type == "HU"),
  table(
    `LLM Competence` = factor(llm_com_round, levels = 1:5),
    `Q Competence`   = factor(q_com_round,   levels = 1:5)
  )
) |> as.data.frame.matrix()
ct_hu_df <- cbind(LLM = rownames(ct_hu_df), ct_hu_df)

ct_hu_df |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(LLM = "LLM Competence (rounded)") |>
  add_header_row(values = c("", "Questionnaire Competence (rounded)"), colwidths = c(1L, 5L)) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "Rows = rounded LLM Competence (1–5); columns = rounded questionnaire Competence (1–5). ",
    "Cell entries are counts. ",
    LABEL_HU, " N = ", sum(d_q_rounded$id_type == "HU"), "."
  ))
```

```{r}
#| label: sec9q-cleanup
#| include: false

```

# 10 Sensitivity: Polychoric Correlations on Rounded Scores

Polychoric correlation treats both variables as ordinal (discretized underlying
latent continua), which is more appropriate than Pearson r when scores are
rounded integers on a 1–5 scale. Both LLM and questionnaire scores are rounded
to the nearest integer (same rounding as Section 9) before computing polychoric
r. The 95% CIs are bootstrap percentile (R = 1,000; polychoric is computationally
heavier than Pearson so a smaller B is used here). Δr shows the difference from
the unrounded Pearson r in @tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff.

```{r}
#| label: sec10-polychoric
#| include: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

set.seed(2026)
d_poly <- d |>
  mutate(
    llm_aut_round = round(llm_aut_mean),
    llm_com_round = round(llm_com_mean),
    llm_rel_round = round(llm_rel_mean),
    q_aut_round   = round(q_aut_mean),
    q_com_round   = round(q_com_mean),
    q_rel_round   = round(q_rel_mean)
  )

llm_round_cols <- c(
  Autonomy    = "llm_aut_round",
  Competence  = "llm_com_round",
  Relatedness = "llm_rel_round"
)
q_round_cols <- c(
  Autonomy    = "q_aut_round",
  Competence  = "q_com_round",
  Relatedness = "q_rel_round"
)

CI_Q_LO     <- (1 - CI_LEVEL) / 2       # = 0.025  lower quantile for bootstrap percentile CIs
CI_Q_HI     <- 1 - (1 - CI_LEVEL) / 2  # = 0.975  upper quantile for bootstrap percentile CIs
POLY_BOOT_R <- 1000  # polychoric is slower to fit; 1000 sufficient for a sensitivity check

poly_boot_ci <- function(df, llm_col, q_col) {
  r_hat   <- as.numeric(psych::polychoric(cbind(df[[llm_col]], df[[q_col]]))$rho[1, 2])
  n       <- nrow(df)
  boot_rs <- replicate(POLY_BOOT_R, {
    db <- df[sample.int(n, replace = TRUE), ]
    tryCatch(
      as.numeric(psych::polychoric(cbind(db[[llm_col]], db[[q_col]]))$rho[1, 2]),
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    )
  })
  boot_rs <- boot_rs[!is.na(boot_rs)]
  list(r     = r_hat,
       ci_lo = unname(quantile(boot_rs, CI_Q_LO)),
       ci_hi = unname(quantile(boot_rs, CI_Q_HI)))
}

r_pearson_ai <- sapply(rq3b_results, `[[`, "r_ai")
r_pearson_hu <- sapply(rq3b_results, `[[`, "r_hu")

poly_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(seq_along(names(llm_round_cols)), function(i) {
  need <- names(llm_round_cols)[[i]]
  ai   <- poly_boot_ci(dplyr::filter(d_poly, id_type == "AI"),
                       llm_round_cols[[need]], q_round_cols[[need]])
  hu   <- poly_boot_ci(dplyr::filter(d_poly, id_type == "HU"),
                       llm_round_cols[[need]], q_round_cols[[need]])
  data.frame(
    Need      = need,
    r_poly_hu = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", hu$r, hu$ci_lo, hu$ci_hi),
    delta_hu  = round(hu$r - r_pearson_hu[[i]], 2),
    r_poly_ai = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", ai$r, ai$ci_lo, ai$ci_hi),
    delta_ai  = round(ai$r - r_pearson_ai[[i]], 2),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-polychoric
#| tbl-cap: "Polychoric Correlations Between Rounded LLM and Questionnaire Scores by Need Dimension and Group (Ordinal Sensitivity Check)"
#| echo: false

poly_tbl |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    Need      = LABEL_NEEDS,
    r_poly_hu = "r_poly [95% CI]",
    delta_hu  = "Δr",
    r_poly_ai = "r_poly [95% CI]",
    delta_ai  = "Δr"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI),
    colwidths = c(1L, 2L, 2L)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "Polychoric correlation between LLM score and questionnaire score, ",
    "both rounded to the nearest integer (same rounding as Section 9). ",
    "95% CIs: bootstrap percentile (R = ", POLY_BOOT_R, "). ",
    "Δr = r_poly − r_Pearson (unrounded, from @tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff). ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ", LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))
```


# 11 Summary: Pearson, Spearman, and Polychoric Correlations

```{r}
#| label: sec11-compute
#| include: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

set.seed(2026)
# d_poly, llm_round_cols, q_round_cols, poly_boot_ci, POLY_BOOT_R reused from section 10

boot_fn_rho <- function(data, i) cor(data$llm[i], data$q[i], method = "spearman")
fmt_ci      <- function(r, lo, hi) sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", r, lo, hi)

# Run all bootstraps once; store raw values for both the table and the plot
method_results <- lapply(seq_along(rq3b_results), function(i) {
  res     <- rq3b_results[[i]]
  need    <- res$need
  llm_col <- need_cols[[need]]
  q_col   <- q_cols[[need]]

  boot_rho_ai <- boot::boot(data.frame(llm = d_grp_ai[[llm_col]],    q = d_grp_ai[[q_col]]),    boot_fn_rho, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
  boot_rho_hu <- boot::boot(data.frame(llm = d_grp_human[[llm_col]], q = d_grp_human[[q_col]]), boot_fn_rho, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
  ci_rho_ai   <- boot::boot.ci(boot_rho_ai, type = "perc")
  ci_rho_hu   <- boot::boot.ci(boot_rho_hu, type = "perc")

  poly_ai <- poly_boot_ci(dplyr::filter(d_poly, id_type == "AI"), llm_round_cols[[need]], q_round_cols[[need]])
  poly_hu <- poly_boot_ci(dplyr::filter(d_poly, id_type == "HU"), llm_round_cols[[need]], q_round_cols[[need]])

  list(
    need        = need,
    r_hu        = res$r_hu,    r_hu_lo  = res$r_hu_ci_lo,       r_hu_hi  = res$r_hu_ci_hi,
    r_ai        = res$r_ai,    r_ai_lo  = res$r_ai_ci_lo,       r_ai_hi  = res$r_ai_ci_hi,
    rho_hu      = res$rho_hu,  rho_hu_lo = ci_rho_hu$percent[4], rho_hu_hi = ci_rho_hu$percent[5],
    rho_ai      = res$rho_ai,  rho_ai_lo = ci_rho_ai$percent[4], rho_ai_hi = ci_rho_ai$percent[5],
    rpoly_hu    = poly_hu$r,   rpoly_hu_lo = poly_hu$ci_lo,      rpoly_hu_hi = poly_hu$ci_hi,
    rpoly_ai    = poly_ai$r,   rpoly_ai_lo = poly_ai$ci_lo,      rpoly_ai_hi = poly_ai$ci_hi
  )
})

method_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(method_results, function(x) {
  data.frame(
    Need        = x$need,
    pearson_hu  = fmt_ci(x$r_hu,     x$r_hu_lo,     x$r_hu_hi),
    spearman_hu = fmt_ci(x$rho_hu,   x$rho_hu_lo,   x$rho_hu_hi),
    poly_hu     = fmt_ci(x$rpoly_hu, x$rpoly_hu_lo, x$rpoly_hu_hi),
    pearson_ai  = fmt_ci(x$r_ai,     x$r_ai_lo,     x$r_ai_hi),
    spearman_ai = fmt_ci(x$rho_ai,   x$rho_ai_lo,   x$rho_ai_hi),
    poly_ai     = fmt_ci(x$rpoly_ai, x$rpoly_ai_lo, x$rpoly_ai_hi),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

need_levels <- c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL)

method_plot_data <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(method_results, function(x) {
  rbind(
    data.frame(need=x$need, group=LABEL_HU, method="Pearson",    r=x$r_hu,     ci_lo=x$r_hu_lo,     ci_hi=x$r_hu_hi,     stringsAsFactors=FALSE),
    data.frame(need=x$need, group=LABEL_AI, method="Pearson",    r=x$r_ai,     ci_lo=x$r_ai_lo,     ci_hi=x$r_ai_hi,     stringsAsFactors=FALSE),
    data.frame(need=x$need, group=LABEL_HU, method="Spearman",   r=x$rho_hu,   ci_lo=x$rho_hu_lo,   ci_hi=x$rho_hu_hi,   stringsAsFactors=FALSE),
    data.frame(need=x$need, group=LABEL_AI, method="Spearman",   r=x$rho_ai,   ci_lo=x$rho_ai_lo,   ci_hi=x$rho_ai_hi,   stringsAsFactors=FALSE),
    data.frame(need=x$need, group=LABEL_HU, method="Polychoric", r=x$rpoly_hu, ci_lo=x$rpoly_hu_lo, ci_hi=x$rpoly_hu_hi, stringsAsFactors=FALSE),
    data.frame(need=x$need, group=LABEL_AI, method="Polychoric", r=x$rpoly_ai, ci_lo=x$rpoly_ai_lo, ci_hi=x$rpoly_ai_hi, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
  )
})) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need   = factor(need,   levels = need_levels),
    group  = factor(group,  levels = c(LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI)),
    method = factor(method, levels = c("Pearson", "Spearman", "Polychoric"))
  )

bracket_data_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(method_results, function(x) {
  need_num <- which(need_levels == x$need)
  rbind(
    data.frame(need_num=need_num, method="Pearson",    r_hu=x$r_hu,     r_ai=x$r_ai,     stringsAsFactors=FALSE),
    data.frame(need_num=need_num, method="Spearman",   r_hu=x$rho_hu,   r_ai=x$rho_ai,   stringsAsFactors=FALSE),
    data.frame(need_num=need_num, method="Polychoric", r_hu=x$rpoly_hu, r_ai=x$rpoly_ai, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
  )
})) |>
  dplyr::mutate(method = factor(method, levels = c("Pearson", "Spearman", "Polychoric")))
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-method-comparison
#| tbl-cap: "LLM–Questionnaire Correlations by Method, Need Dimension, and Group"
#| echo: false

method_tbl |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  set_header_labels(
    Need        = LABEL_NEEDS,
    pearson_hu  = "Pearson r",
    spearman_hu = "Spearman ρ",
    poly_hu     = "Polychoric r",
    pearson_ai  = "Pearson r",
    spearman_ai = "Spearman ρ",
    poly_ai     = "Polychoric r"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI),
    colwidths = c(1L, 3L, 3L)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "All values shown as r [95% CI]. ",
    "Pearson r and Spearman ρ computed on unrounded continuous scores; ",
    "Pearson CIs: bootstrap percentile (R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "); ",
    "Spearman CIs: bootstrap percentile (R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "). ",
    "Polychoric r computed on rounded integer scores; ",
    "CIs: bootstrap percentile (R = ", POLY_BOOT_R, "). ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ", LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-method-comparison
#| fig-cap: "LLM–questionnaire correlations by method, need dimension, and group. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs. Yellow bracket = Δr (AI − Human). Pearson and Spearman on unrounded scores; Polychoric on rounded integer scores."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 500

DODGE_W_11 <- 0.1
TICK_W_11  <- 0.10

fig11_data <- method_plot_data |>
  dplyr::mutate(x = as.numeric(need) + ifelse(group == LABEL_HU, -DODGE_W_11, DODGE_W_11))

group_colors_11 <- c(setNames(COL_AI_SHADES[1], LABEL_AI), setNames(COL_HU_SHADES[1], LABEL_HU))

ggplot(fig11_data, aes(x = x, y = r, color = group, fill = group, group = group)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5, color = "grey60") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.5, stroke = 1.8) +
  geom_segment(data = bracket_data_all,
    aes(x = need_num, xend = need_num, y = r_hu, yend = r_ai),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(data = bracket_data_all,
    aes(x = need_num - TICK_W_11, xend = need_num + TICK_W_11, y = r_ai, yend = r_ai),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(data = bracket_data_all,
    aes(x = need_num - TICK_W_11, xend = need_num + TICK_W_11, y = r_hu, yend = r_hu),
    color = COL_DIFF, linewidth = 0.25, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks   = 1:3,
    labels   = need_levels,
    limits   = c(0.6, 3.6),
    position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors_11, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = group_colors_11, name = NULL) +
  facet_wrap(~ method, nrow = 1) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Correlation") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text                 = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    panel.grid.major.x   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x   = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x.top      = element_text(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.y   = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y   = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.y         = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    strip.background     = element_blank(),
    strip.text           = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position      = c(0.06, 0.18),
    legend.direction     = "horizontal",
    legend.margin        = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key           = element_blank()
  )
```

*Note.* Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs. Pearson and Spearman: R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`; Polychoric: R = `r POLY_BOOT_R`. AI surrogates N = `r nrow(d_grp_ai)`; Humans N = `r nrow(d_grp_human)`. Yellow bracket = Δr (AI − Human).

```{r}
#| label: sec11-cleanup
#| include: false

```

# 12 Sensitivity: Actual Word Count as a Comparability Covariate

The matched AI condition (three texts, ~150-word target) approximates the human sample's average total word count. However, human participants wrote Swedish texts with a wider and generally higher distribution of actual word counts compared to the tightly constrained English AI texts. If LLM scores are sensitive to raw text length, between-group differences in Sections 7 and 8 may partly reflect this mismatch.

Two analyses address this. 

First, word count distributions are visualised for both groups (expanding Section 3.6). 

Second, within-group regressions of each LLM score on `actual_words` are reported separately per group and need dimension. If the slopes and R² values are near zero, word count does not appreciably drive LLM scores within either group, and the between-group comparison is not confounded by the word count gap. Third, partial correlations for RQ3b partial out `actual_words` before assessing LLM–questionnaire correspondence.

## 12.1 Word Count Distributions by Group

```{r}
#| label: fig-wordcount-dist
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of actual word counts (total across scored texts) for AI surrogates and human participants. Vertical lines indicate group medians. The difference in location and spread reflects that human texts were unconstrained and written in Swedish, while AI surrogate texts were constrained to a target length in English."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

wc_plot_data <- d |>
  mutate(Group = factor(c(AI = LABEL_AI, HU = LABEL_HU)[as.character(id_type)],
                        levels = c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU)))

wc_medians <- wc_plot_data |>
  group_by(Group) |>
  summarise(Mdn = median(actual_words), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(wc_plot_data, aes(x = actual_words, fill = Group, color = Group)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_rug(alpha = 0.25, sides = "b") +
  geom_vline(data = wc_medians, aes(xintercept = Mdn, color = Group, linetype = Group),
             linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c(COL_AI_LINE, COL_HU_LINE), c(LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU))) +
  labs(x = "Actual word count (total across scored texts)", y = "Density",
       fill = LABEL_SAMPLE, color = LABEL_SAMPLE, linetype = "Median") +
  theme_classic(base_family = THESIS_FONT, base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

rm(wc_plot_data, wc_medians)
```

## 12.2 RQ3a Sensitivity: Within-Group Regression of LLM Scores on Word Count

For each group and need dimension, a simple linear regression predicts the LLM score from `actual_words`. A near-zero slope and low R² indicate that word count does not drive LLM scores within that group; the between-group comparison in Section 7 is then not confounded by the word count gap.

```{r}
#| label: rq3a-wc-regression
#| include: false

wc_reg_results <- lapply(names(need_cols), function(need_label) {
  col <- need_cols[[need_label]]

  fit_ai <- lm(as.formula(paste(col, "~ actual_words")), data = d_grp_ai)
  fit_hu <- lm(as.formula(paste(col, "~ actual_words")), data = d_grp_human)

  s_ai <- summary(fit_ai)
  s_hu <- summary(fit_hu)

  data.frame(
    need     = need_label,
    b_ai     = coef(fit_ai)[["actual_words"]],
    se_ai    = s_ai$coefficients["actual_words", "Std. Error"],
    p_ai     = s_ai$coefficients["actual_words", "Pr(>|t|)"],
    r2_ai    = s_ai$r.squared,
    b_hu     = coef(fit_hu)[["actual_words"]],
    se_hu    = s_hu$coefficients["actual_words", "Std. Error"],
    p_hu     = s_hu$coefficients["actual_words", "Pr(>|t|)"],
    r2_hu    = s_hu$r.squared,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

wc_reg_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(wc_reg_results)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq3a-wc-regression
#| tbl-cap: "Within-Group Regression of LLM Scores on Actual Word Count by Need Dimension (RQ3a Word Count Sensitivity)"
#| echo: false

wc_reg_df |>
  transmute(
    Need         = need,
    `b (AI)`     = round(b_ai,  4),
    `SE (AI)`    = round(se_ai, 4),
    `p (AI)`     = fmt_p(p_ai),
    `R² (AI)`    = round(r2_ai, 3),
    `b (Human)`  = round(b_hu,  4),
    `SE (Human)` = round(se_hu, 4),
    `p (Human)`  = fmt_p(p_hu),
    `R² (Human)` = round(r2_hu, 3)
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU),
    colwidths = c(1L, 4L, 4L)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "OLS regression: LLM score ~ actual_words, fitted separately within each group. ",
    "b = unstandardized slope (change in LLM score per additional word). ",
    "R² = proportion of variance in LLM score explained by word count within that group. ",
    "Near-zero b and R² indicate word count does not drive LLM scores within the group. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ", LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))

rm(wc_reg_results, wc_reg_df)
```

## 12.3 RQ3b Sensitivity: Partial Correlations Controlling for Word Count

Within each group, both the LLM score and the questionnaire score are regressed on `actual_words`; the partial correlation is computed on the residuals. Specifically, both variables are individually regressed on `actual_words` via OLS; the Pearson correlation of the two residual vectors is then taken. This residualization approach is equivalent to a formal partial correlation when both regressions share the same single covariate and OLS is used — the values are numerically identical. This removes variance attributable to text length from both measures before assessing their correspondence. Bootstrap CIs use the same stratified resampling as Section 8.

```{r}
#| label: rq3b-partial-corr
#| include: false

set.seed(2026)
boot_fn_partial_r <- function(data, i) {
  db      <- data[i, ]
  resid_l <- residuals(lm(llm ~ actual_words, data = db))
  resid_q <- residuals(lm(q   ~ actual_words, data = db))
  cor(resid_l, resid_q, method = "pearson")
}

partial_results <- lapply(names(need_cols), function(need_label) {
  llm_col <- need_cols[[need_label]]
  q_col   <- q_cols[[need_label]]

  dat_ai <- data.frame(llm = d_grp_ai[[llm_col]], q = d_grp_ai[[q_col]],
                       actual_words = d_grp_ai$actual_words)
  dat_hu <- data.frame(llm = d_grp_human[[llm_col]], q = d_grp_human[[q_col]],
                       actual_words = d_grp_human$actual_words)

  pr_ai <- cor(residuals(lm(llm ~ actual_words, dat_ai)),
               residuals(lm(q   ~ actual_words, dat_ai)))
  pr_hu <- cor(residuals(lm(llm ~ actual_words, dat_hu)),
               residuals(lm(q   ~ actual_words, dat_hu)))

  boot_ai <- boot::boot(dat_ai, boot_fn_partial_r, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
  boot_hu <- boot::boot(dat_hu, boot_fn_partial_r, R = BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES)
  ci_ai   <- boot::boot.ci(boot_ai, type = "perc")
  ci_hu   <- boot::boot.ci(boot_hu, type = "perc")

  raw_res <- Filter(function(x) x$need == need_label, rq3b_results)[[1]]

  data.frame(
    need        = need_label,
    pr_ai       = pr_ai,
    pr_ai_ci_lo = ci_ai$percent[4],
    pr_ai_ci_hi = ci_ai$percent[5],
    pr_hu       = pr_hu,
    pr_hu_ci_lo = ci_hu$percent[4],
    pr_hu_ci_hi = ci_hu$percent[5],
    raw_r_ai    = raw_res$r_ai,
    raw_r_hu    = raw_res$r_hu,
    delta_ai    = pr_ai - raw_res$r_ai,
    delta_hu    = pr_hu - raw_res$r_hu,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

partial_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(partial_results)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq3b-partial
#| tbl-cap: "Partial Correlations Between LLM Scores and Questionnaire Scores Controlling for Actual Word Count by Need Dimension and Group (RQ3b Word Count Sensitivity)"
#| echo: false

partial_df |>
  transmute(
    Need        = need,
    `r (Human)` = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", pr_hu, pr_hu_ci_lo, pr_hu_ci_hi),
    `Δr (HU)`   = round(delta_hu, 2),
    `r (AI)`    = sprintf("%.2f [%.2f, %.2f]", pr_ai, pr_ai_ci_lo, pr_ai_ci_hi),
    `Δr (AI)`   = round(delta_ai, 2)
  ) |>
  flextable() |>
  style_apa7() |>
  colformat_double(digits = 2) |>
  set_header_labels(
    `r (Human)` = paste0("pr [", CI_LABEL, "]"),
    `Δr (HU)`   = "Δr",
    `r (AI)`    = paste0("pr [", CI_LABEL, "]"),
    `Δr (AI)`   = "Δr"
  ) |>
  add_header_row(
    values    = c("", LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI),
    colwidths = c(1L, 2L, 2L)
  ) |>
  add_apa_note(paste0(
    "pr = partial Pearson correlation between LLM score and questionnaire score, ",
    "with actual word count partialled out via residualization within each group. ",
    "CIs: 95% bootstrap percentile (R = ", BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES, "). ",
    "Δr = pr − r(unrounded Pearson); reference values from @tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff. ",
    "Positive Δr indicates the partial correlation exceeds the raw correlation (word count suppressed the association); ",
    "negative Δr indicates word count inflated the raw correlation. ",
    LABEL_AI, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_ai), "; ", LABEL_HU, " N = ", nrow(d_grp_human), "."
  ))
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-rq3b-partial
#| fig-cap: "Partial Pearson correlations (controlling for actual word count) between LLM scores and questionnaire scores by need dimension and group (RQ3b). Open symbols with error bars = partial r (this section); filled symbols = raw Pearson r from @tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff for reference. Error bars = 95% bootstrap CIs for partial r."
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 6
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-dpi: 300

DODGE_W_12 <- 0.12

partial_plot_data <- partial_df |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    need = factor(need, levels = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL))
  ) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(pr_ai, pr_hu),
    names_to  = "group_key",
    values_to = "pr"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    group = ifelse(group_key == "pr_ai", LABEL_AI, LABEL_HU),
    ci_lo = ifelse(group_key == "pr_ai", pr_ai_ci_lo, pr_hu_ci_lo),
    ci_hi = ifelse(group_key == "pr_ai", pr_ai_ci_hi, pr_hu_ci_hi),
    raw_r = ifelse(group_key == "pr_ai", raw_r_ai, raw_r_hu),
    x     = as.numeric(need) + ifelse(group == LABEL_HU, -DODGE_W_12, DODGE_W_12),
    x_raw = as.numeric(need) + ifelse(group == LABEL_HU, -DODGE_W_12 * 2, DODGE_W_12 * 2),
    group = factor(group, levels = c(LABEL_HU, LABEL_AI))
  )

group_colors_12 <- c(setNames(COL_AI_SHADES[1], LABEL_AI),
                     setNames(COL_HU_SHADES[1], LABEL_HU))

ggplot(partial_plot_data, aes(x = x, color = group, fill = group)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.4, color = "grey60") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), width = 0, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(y = pr), shape = 21, fill = "white", size = 1.8, stroke = 1.8) +
  geom_point(aes(x = x_raw, y = raw_r), shape = 16, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.35) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks   = 1:3,
    labels   = c(TABLE_LABEL_AUT, TABLE_LABEL_COM, TABLE_LABEL_REL),
    limits   = c(0.6, 3.6),
    position = "top"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors_12, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = group_colors_12, name = NULL) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Correlation") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = THESIS_FONTSIZE) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = THESIS_FONT),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x.top    = element_text(colour = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.3),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12)),
    legend.position    = c(0.18, 0.15),
    legend.direction   = "horizontal",
    legend.margin      = margin(t = 4),
    legend.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key         = element_blank()
  )
```

*Note.* Open symbols with error bars = partial r [95% bootstrap CI, R = `r BOOTSTRAP_SAMPLES`]. Filled symbols = raw Pearson r from @tbl-rq3b-fisher-zdiff (no CI shown). AI surrogates N = `r nrow(d_grp_ai)`; Humans N = `r nrow(d_grp_human)`.

```{r}
#| label: session-info
#| include: false
sessionInfo()
```
